docs.title: Paradise Bot
docs.text: The official wiki for Paradise Bot.

docs.title: THE OFFICIAL WIKI
docs.text:
Paradise Bot is a feature-rich Discord bot covering gambling, economy, multiplayer games, farming, properties, pets, and more. Use this wiki to look up any command, mechanic, or feature.

Quick links:

  • About Paradise Bot
  • Rules
  • FAQ
  • Settings
  • Support

docs.title: ECONOMY & PROGRESSION
docs.text:
The core gameplay loop - earn, save, spend, invest.

  • Money Management - /balance, /deposit, /withdraw, /pay, /networth, /leaderboard
  • Earning Money - /daily, /hourly, /weekly, /beg, /vote
  • Crime System - /crime, /crimestats
  • Robbery - /rob
  • Banking System - Interest, capacity, taxes
  • Voting System - Daily votes, streaks, rewards
  • Lottery - Daily drawings
  • Scratch Tickets - Open, redeem, trade

docs.title: Casino Games
docs.text:
Single-player gambling games - table games, slots, dice.

  • All Casino Games - Baccarat, Blackjack, Poker, Roulette, Slots, Crash, Tower, Mines, Plinko, Wheel, Dice, Coinflip, and more

docs.title: Multiplayer & Social
docs.text:
Head-to-head matches and group games.

  • Multiplayer Games - Chess, Battleship, Connect 4, Tic Tac Toe, Wordle, duels, combat

  • Fishing - Cast and catch
  • Restaurants - Fries

docs.title: In Closing
docs.text: Thanks for reading. Paradise Bot is built and maintained by one developer with a small staff team for support - your engagement, votes, and Patreon support are what keep it alive.

docs.title: Quick reminders
docs.text:
• Voting is the easiest way to support the bot for free - and it gives you scratch cards in return.
  • Patreon unlocks permanent in-game perks across every system. See Patreon Shop.
  • Bug reports and suggestions genuinely shape the bot. Use /bugreport and /suggest.
  • Have fun. This is a simulated casino - no real money is at stake. Don't take losses personally.

docs.title: Stay connected
docs.text:
• Discord: discord.gg/vMcbru7qMV
  • Github: Daniel-191/Paradise
  • Patreon: patreon.com/ParadiseBotx

docs.title: Final word
docs.text:
If something in this wiki is wrong, outdated, or missing, please file a bug report or open a Github issue. The wiki is meant to stay in sync with the code - when it doesn't, that's a bug.

Good luck out there. 🎰

docs.title: Crime System
docs.text: Pull off heists for high-reward income - at the cost of police heat and the chance of losing money on a botched job. Four crime tiers from low-risk pickpocketing up to high-stakes crypto exchange hacks.

docs.title: How crimes work
docs.text:
Run /crime to open the operations panel and pick a target. Each crime plays out across multiple animated phases (3–5 phases depending on tier) before resolving as a success or failure. There's also a bonus chance on every success that multiplies your payout.

Use /crimestats to study odds and payouts before you commit.

docs.title: Crime tiers
docs.text:
Crime Success Payout Loss on fail Bonus chance Bonus mult Heat Pickpocket 85% $500K – $1.2M $10K – $50K 25% 2.5× 1 Bank Heist 65% $5M – $15M $200K – $500K 15% 4× 4 Casino Heist 55% $8M – $25M $300K – $800K 12% 5× 4 Crypto Hack 35% $25M – $100M $1M – $3M 3% 10× 5

Heat level is informational - it represents police attention, not a hard mechanic.

docs.title: Expected value
docs.text:
Quick reference for picking a crime by EV:

EV = (success_rate × avg_reward) - (fail_rate × avg_loss)
Crime EV (no bonus) Pickpocket ~$719K Bank Heist ~$6.4M Casino Heist ~$8.9M Crypto Hack ~$20M

Bonuses push these higher; the listed EV is conservative.

docs.title: Commands
docs.text:
• /crime - Run a crime
  • /crimestats - Browse odds and payouts before committing

docs.title: Cooldowns and tax
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 1 hour (Patreon T3+ reduces to ~39 minutes).
  • A portion of crime profits is taxed and added to the lottery pool - your crimes fund the community jackpot.

docs.title: /crime
docs.text: Run a criminal operation. Pick from four risk tiers and watch your heist play out in real-time animated phases.

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Opens the Criminal Operations panel where you select a target tier (Pickpocket, Bank, Casino, or Crypto). Each crime runs through several phases - scouting, infiltration, execution - before resolving. Successes pay big and have a chance to roll a bonus multiplier; failures lose you a smaller amount and put your cooldown back on the clock.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /crime

docs.title: Pickpocket - Low Risk
docs.text:
• Success rate: 85%
  • Reward: $500,000 – $1,200,000
  • Loss on fail: $10,000 – $50,000
  • Bonus: 25% chance, 2.5× multiplier
  • Phases: Scouting → Positioning → Execution
  • Best for steady income with minimal downside.

docs.title: Bank Heist - High Risk
docs.text:
• Success rate: 65%
  • Reward: $5M – $15M
  • Loss on fail: $200K – $500K
  • Bonus: 15% chance, 4× multiplier
  • Phases: Infiltration → System Bypass → Vault Access → Asset Extraction → Escape

docs.title: Casino Heist - High Risk
docs.text:
• Success rate: 55%
  • Reward: $8M – $25M
  • Loss on fail: $300K – $800K
  • Bonus: 12% chance, 5× multiplier
  • Phases: Floor Entry → Target Positioning → System Infiltration → Operation Execution → Cash Conversion

docs.title: Crypto Hack - Extreme Risk
docs.text:
• Success rate: 35%
  • Reward: $25M – $100M
  • Loss on fail: $1M – $3M
  • Bonus: 3% chance, 10× multiplier (huge payout if it lands)
  • Phases: Network Recon → Firewall Penetration → Key Extraction → Cold Storage Access → Asset Transfer

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 1 hour. Patreon T3+ reduces to ~39 minutes (T1: 10%, T2: 20%, T3+: 35%).
  • The cooldown is set the moment you open the panel - even if you don't pick a crime. Choose carefully.
  • A portion of every successful crime goes to the lottery pool tax.
  • Use /crimestats to compare odds and payouts before running.
  • See /cooldowns to check when your next crime is available.

docs.title: /crimestats
docs.text: Browse the success rates, payouts, and risk profiles of every crime type without committing to one.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Opens the Criminal Intelligence panel - a read-only view of all four crime tiers with detailed breakdowns: success probability, reward range, loss range, bonus chance, bonus multiplier, heat level, and phase-by-phase descriptions.

This is the planning tool. Use it to figure out which crime fits your risk tolerance before running /crime.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /crimestats

docs.title: What it shows
docs.text:
For each crime type:

  • Success Rate - % chance the crime succeeds
  • Reward Range - min/max money payout on success
  • Loss Range - min/max money loss on failure
  • Bonus Event Chance - % chance a successful crime rolls a bonus multiplier
  • Bonus Multiplier - how much the bonus event multiplies the reward
  • Heat Level - police attention indicator (1–5 scale, informational)
  • Phases - each step the operation goes through

docs.title: Expected Value formula
docs.text:
EV = (success_rate × avg_reward) - (fail_rate × avg_loss)

Use this to compare crimes objectively. Bonus events skew real-world EV higher than the base formula.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• No cooldown - check anytime, doesn't affect your /crime cooldown.
  • This is purely informational; it doesn't run any crime or change your stats.
  • Pair with /crime once you've decided on a target.

docs.title: Earning Money
docs.text: Free money sources - no risk, no gambling required. The recurring rewards (/daily, /hourly, /weekly, /monthly) plus /beg and /vote are how you build a starting bankroll.

docs.title: Commands at a glance
docs.text:
Command Cooldown Base reward /hourly 1 hour $7.5M + streak bonus /daily 24 hours $75M /weekly 7 days $375M /monthly 30 days $2B /beg 15 seconds $10K – $100K (random) /vote 12 hours per site $50M (scales with streak) /vote-milestones - View streak unlocks

Use /cooldowns to see when each is ready.

docs.title: Multipliers stack
docs.text: Every recurring reward (/daily, /weekly, /monthly, /hourly) gets multiplied by two independent boosters:

docs.title: Vote streak multiplier
docs.text: Streak Multiplier Tier 1+ 1.25× Newcomer 7+ 1.5× Starting 14+ 1.75× Building 21+ 2× Growing 30+ 2.5× Rising 45+ 2.75× Loyal 60+ 3× Regular 75+ 3.5× Committed 100+ 4× Dedicated 125+ 4.5× Advanced 150+ 5× Veteran 175+ 5.5× Expert 200+ 6× Master 250+ 7× Elite 300+ 8× Diamond 356+ 10× Legendary

docs.title: Patreon multiplier (DWM)
docs.text:
Tier Multiplier Donator (T1) 2.5× Chill (T2) 5× Epic (T3) 10× Super (T4) 15× Mega (T5) 20× Ultimate (T6) 25× Crazy (T7) 30× Godly (T8) 40×

A Godly patron with a Legendary streak earns 400× the base reward - /monthly becomes $800B per claim.

What patrons don't get

Patreon does not reduce cooldowns on /daily, /weekly, /monthly, or /hourly - those stay fixed. Cooldown reductions apply to other commands like /beg, /crime, /rob, etc.

docs.title: /beg
docs.text: Beg the streets for a small handout.

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Pays a random amount between $10,000 and $100,000 every 15 seconds. The smallest income source in the bot, but it has the shortest cooldown - useful when you've burned through your wallet and need anything to keep playing.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /beg

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 15 seconds.
  • Reward: random $10K – $100K (uniform distribution).
  • Patreon supporters get a 35% cooldown reduction on this command (~10s instead of 15s).
  • No multipliers - vote streak and Patreon tier do not boost the amount.
  • For bigger income at longer cooldowns, see /hourly, /daily, /crime.

docs.title: /daily
docs.text: Claim your once-a-day money reward.

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Pays out $75,000,000 base, multiplied by your vote streak and Patreon tier if you have either. Available once every 24 hours. The response shows the full reward breakdown so you can see exactly where the multipliers came from.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /daily

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 24 hours.
  • Base reward: $75M.
  • Multipliers: vote streak (1.25× to 10×) and Patreon (up to 40×). They stack.
  • The cooldown is not reduced by Patreon for this command.
  • Boost your reward by maintaining a vote streak - see /vote.
  • Use /cooldowns to check when your next claim is available.

docs.title: /hourly
docs.text: Hourly paycheck with a streak bonus that grows the longer you keep collecting on time.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Pays $7,500,000 base, plus $250,000 per hour of streak (up to 24 hours of streak), all multiplied by your vote streak. Maintain the hourly streak by claiming within 2 hours of the previous claim - miss the window and your streak resets to 1.

At max streak (24 hours), the streak bonus alone is $6M, bringing the unmultiplied reward to $13.5M per hour.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /hourly

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 1 hour.
  • Base reward: $7.5M.
  • Streak bonus: +$250K per streak hour, capped at 24.
  • Grace period: 2 hours. Claim within 2h of last claim to keep streak; miss it and streak resets to 1.
  • Vote streak multiplier applies to the total (base + streak bonus).
  • Patreon does not reduce this command's cooldown.
  • See [/hourly-stats] for full streak history (separate command).

docs.title: /monthly
docs.text: The biggest single recurring payout - a once-per-month windfall.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Pays out $2,000,000,000 base, multiplied by your vote streak and Patreon tier. Available once every 30 days.

This is the largest recurring reward in the bot. With max multipliers (40× Patreon × 10× streak) it pays $800B per claim.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /monthly

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 30 days.
  • Base reward: $2B.
  • Multipliers: vote streak (up to 10×) × Patreon (up to 40×). Stacks for up to 400×.
  • Patreon does not reduce this command's cooldown - set a calendar reminder.
  • Don't forget to claim before reset on day 30 - the cooldown is wall-clock, not from-last-claim.

docs.title: /vote-milestones
docs.text: View the permanent unlocks you get for maintaining a vote streak.

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Shows your current vote streak alongside three milestone unlocks. Each milestone is active only while your streak is at or above the threshold - drop below and you lose the perk (and in the case of property slots, may have properties auto-sold).

docs.title: Milestones
docs.text: Streak Unlock What it does 10 days Property Protection 70% fewer random property events (taxes, repairs, etc.) 15 days Extra Property Slots +5 property slots (15 total instead of 10) 30 days Extra Farming Slot +1 permanent farming slot

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /vote-milestones

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 3 seconds.
  • Milestones are active, not earned-once. Drop below the threshold and the bonus turns off.
  • Property slot warning: if your streak drops below 15 and you currently own more than 10 properties, the excess will be auto-sold at 70% refund value. Don't let your streak break if you're using the extra slots.
  • Farming slot: the 30-day farming slot is added/removed automatically as your streak crosses 30.
  • Streak is maintained by voting at least once every 12 hours on Top.gg or DBL - see /vote.

docs.title: /vote
docs.text: Vote for Paradise Bot on listing sites for money, bonuses, and a streak that boosts every other reward.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Opens the vote panel with buttons for each supported listing site. Each site has its own 12-hour cooldown, so you can vote on multiple sites per day. Voting:

  • Pays a money reward that scales with your current streak
  • Boosts your farm's growth speed for 1.5 hours
  • Cuts your /rob cooldown by 50%
  • Awards 1 scratch card
  • Voting on both Top.gg + DBL within 24h grants a +2 daily heist limit bonus

Maintaining the streak (vote at least once every 12h on either site) compounds into the streak multiplier that boosts every recurring reward - see the Earning Money index for the full multiplier table.

docs.title: Supported sites
docs.text:
• Top.gg - main listing site
  • DBL (DiscordBotList) - secondary listing
  • Rank.top - bonus site (counts toward streak)

Each site is on its own 12-hour cooldown.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /vote

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown (slash command itself): 3 seconds. Each listing site has a separate 12-hour cooldown.
  • Base reward: $50M, scaled by streak bonus and Patreon multiplier.
  • Streak rule: vote at least once every 12 hours on either Top.gg or DBL to maintain the streak.
  • Streak rewards calculation uses tiered scaling - early days add ~20% per day, later days add up to +1.5× per day.
  • Milestone bonuses are paid on top of streak rewards at days 7, 30, 90, 180, and 365 (2×, 10×, 30×, 60×, 150× of base, respectively).
  • Patreon vote multiplier is separate from the DWM multiplier: T1 = 1.0×, T2 = 1.5×, T3 = 2×, T4 = 3×, T5 = 4×, T6 = 5×, T7 = 6.5×, T8 = 15×.
  • See /vote-milestones for streak-tier permanent unlocks (property protection, extra slots).
  • You can toggle a vote-ready DM reminder from the same panel.

docs.title: /weekly
docs.text: The weekly money reward - bigger than /daily, with the same multiplier system.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Pays out $375,000,000 base, multiplied by your vote streak and Patreon tier. Available once every 7 days.

A typical free user with a 30-day vote streak earns $937.5M per claim. A Godly patron with a Legendary streak earns up to $15B.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /weekly

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 7 days.
  • Base reward: $375M.
  • Multipliers: vote streak (up to 10×) × Patreon (up to 40×).
  • Patreon does not reduce this command's cooldown.
  • Pair with /monthly and /daily for the full reward stack.

docs.title: Money Management
docs.text: Everything you need to check your balance, move money between your wallet and bank, send money to other players, and track where your money has gone.

docs.title: Wallet vs Bank
docs.text:
Paradise Bot splits your money into two places:

  • Wallet - Money you can spend, gamble, or send. Wallet money is at risk: it can be stolen via /rob and lost in casino games.
  • Bank - Safe storage. Bank money cannot be robbed or gambled directly. Money in your bank earns daily interest at 11:00 UTC. Bank capacity is limited; upgrade with /shop.

docs.title: Commands at a glance
docs.text: Command What it does /balance Check your wallet and bank totals /deposit Move money wallet → bank /withdraw Move money bank → wallet /pay Send money to another player /networth Full wealth breakdown including properties and farm assets /leaderboard Top 100 richest players (global or server) /transactions View your transaction history /transactions_toggle Block or allow incoming payments /cooldowns See when income commands are ready again /stats Profit/loss across every game you've played

docs.title: Amount shortcuts
docs.text:
Most money commands accept shorthand for big numbers:

  • 1k → 1,000
  • 5m → 5,000,000
  • 2.5b → 2,500,000,000
  • 1t → 1,000,000,000,000
  • max → all of the relevant balance
  • half → half of it

docs.title: /balance
docs.text: Check the wallet and bank balance of yourself or another player.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Shows your current wallet and bank totals side-by-side, your bank capacity, and any patron tier badge you have. If you check yourself, you'll also see vote prompts when a vote is available and reminders for upcoming voting milestones.

Bank capacity comes from your /shop upgrades plus a Patreon bonus if you support the bot.

docs.title: Optional Fields
docs.text: • user - Mention another server member to view their balance instead of your own.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /balance [user]

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /balance /balance @username

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 3 seconds.
  • Daily interest is paid into your bank at 11:00 UTC.
  • Viewing someone else's balance does not trigger interest updates for them - only for yourself.
  • Bank money cannot be robbed or gambled directly. Move money with /deposit and /withdraw.

docs.title: /cooldowns
docs.text: See when each of your income commands will be ready to use again.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Shows the remaining cooldown on every recurring command in one place: /daily, /weekly, /monthly, /hourly, /beg, /mine, /crime, /rob, /heist, and weekly scratch tickets. Ready commands are marked with a green toggle; commands still on cooldown show the time remaining.

/cd is a shorter alias for the same command.

docs.title: Optional Fields
docs.text: • user - Mention another player to view their cooldowns instead of your own.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /cooldowns [user] /cd [user]

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /cooldowns /cd /cooldowns @username

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• No cooldown - check as often as you like.
  • Patreon supporters get 35% faster cooldowns on most commands.
  • Heist cooldowns are tracked separately and can be hours long after a successful run.
  • Weekly scratches reset every 7 days; the page shows when your next batch unlocks.

docs.title: /deposit
docs.text: Move money from your wallet into your bank, where it's safe from robbery and gambling losses.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Deposits the requested amount from your wallet into your bank balance. The amount is capped by both your wallet balance and your remaining bank capacity. Once banked, money is protected and starts earning daily interest at the next 11:00 UTC payout.

If your bank is full, upgrade your vault with /shop - bigger vaults hold more money and earn more interest.

docs.title: Required Fields
docs.text:
• amount - How much to deposit. Accepts plain numbers (5000), shorthand (5k, 2.5m, 1b), or keywords:
    • max - Deposit as much as your wallet and bank capacity allow.
    • half - Deposit half of your wallet (subject to bank capacity).

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /deposit [amount]

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /deposit 10000 /deposit 5m /deposit max

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 3 seconds.
  • Minimum deposit: $100.
  • If the requested amount exceeds your bank's remaining space, you'll be told exactly how much you can fit and prompted to upgrade your vault.
  • Every deposit is recorded in /transactions.

docs.title: /leaderboard
docs.text: The top 100 richest players, ranked by net worth.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Shows a paginated leaderboard of the top 100 richest players, either globally or restricted to this server. Net worth is wallet + bank + property value, so just hoarding money isn't the only path to the top.

The footer shows your own rank, even if you're outside the top 100. The global top 10 also receive a special Discord role automatically (where the role exists in the server).

docs.title: Optional Fields
docs.text:
• scope - Which leaderboard to view:
    • global (default) - Every Paradise Bot user, everywhere.
    • server - Only members of the current server.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /leaderboard [scope]

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /leaderboard /leaderboard global /leaderboard server

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 10 seconds (longer than other balance commands because the leaderboard is heavier to compute).
  • Results are cached for performance; rankings update every few minutes, not in real time.
  • The "Top 10" Discord role is granted/removed automatically based on the global leaderboard.
  • Your rank is shown in the footer regardless of whether you appear in the top 100.

docs.title: /networth
docs.text: Detailed wealth breakdown including liquid assets, properties, and farm assets.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Calculates your true total wealth by combining wallet, bank, the value of every property you own, and the value of your farm (planted crops plus harvested crop inventory). The result is broken down into liquid assets (wallet + bank), property assets, and farm assets, with the percentage each represents of your overall net worth and your global rank.

Farm assets count your currently planted crops at their sell price (regardless of whether they've finished growing yet) plus any harvested crops sitting in your inventory, so your net worth and rank don't temporarily dip just because your gold is tied up in crops waiting to be harvested.

Useful for seeing how much of your wealth is tied up in real estate, farming, vs. spendable money.

docs.title: Optional Fields
docs.text: • user - Mention another server member to view their net worth instead of your own.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /networth [user]

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /networth /networth @username

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 3 seconds.
  • Net worth = wallet + bank + total purchase value of owned properties + farm assets.
  • Farm assets = (planted crops × their sell price) + (harvested crop inventory × sell price). Crops are valued at full sell price even while still growing, so planting doesn't cost you rank while you wait to harvest.
  • Top 5 most valuable properties are listed individually; the rest are grouped under "Other Properties."
  • Your rank is your position among all users globally (not just this server).
  • Net worth is the metric used for the /leaderboard.

docs.title: /pay
docs.text: Send wallet money to another player.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Pays another user from your wallet (not your bank). You'll get a confirmation prompt before the transfer goes through, and the amount, recipient, and any reason are written to your transaction history.

Both you and the recipient must have transactions enabled (see /transactions_toggle) and neither of you can be under payment restrictions from the robbery system.

docs.title: Required Fields
docs.text:
• user - The Discord member to send money to. Cannot be yourself or a bot.
  • amount - How much to send. Accepts plain numbers, shorthand (k, m, b, t), or keywords (max, half).

docs.title: Optional Fields
docs.text: • reason - A short note attached to the payment. Shown on the confirmation prompt and stored with the transaction.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /pay [user] [amount] [reason]

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /pay @username 5000 /pay @username 1m gift /pay @username max paying back what I owe

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 3 seconds.
  • Minimum payment: $1. Maximum: 10 quadrillion ($10,000,000,000,000,000).
  • A confirmation button appears before the payment goes through. The transaction is re-checked at confirmation time, so balances and payment-block status are validated twice.
  • You cannot pay yourself or a bot.
  • If you've recently been robbed (or robbed someone), you may be temporarily unable to send or receive payments. Use /cooldowns to check.
  • The recipient must have payments enabled - they can disable receiving with /transactions_toggle.

docs.title: /stats
docs.text: Profit and losses across every game you've played.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Shows your gambling track record: how much you've won, how much you've lost, and your net result for each game. Without arguments, you get a paginated list of every game you've touched. Pass a specific game to drill into just that one.

A green chart icon means you're up overall on that game; red means you're down.

docs.title: Optional Fields
docs.text:
• member - Check another player's stats instead of your own.
  • game - Show stats for a single game (autocompletes from games you've actually played).

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /stats [member] [game]

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /stats /stats game:blackjack /stats @username /stats @username game:slots

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 5 seconds.
  • Profit and losses are tracked per game, separately. Net = profit − losses.
  • If a player has no recorded games yet, you'll see a "no stats found" embed.
  • For ranking by total wealth instead of game results, use /leaderboard.

docs.title: /transactions_toggle
docs.text: Block or allow other players from sending you money.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Disabling transactions means no one can send you money via /pay. You'll also be unable to send payments yourself while disabled. Useful if you want to opt out of trading entirely or block unwanted gifts.

The setting persists until you change it.

docs.title: Required Fields
docs.text: • setting - enable or disable.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /transactions_toggle [setting]

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /transactions_toggle enable /transactions_toggle disable

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 3 seconds.
  • Setting is saved per-user and applies to all servers, not just the one you ran it in.
  • While disabled, both incoming and outgoing payments are blocked.
  • This setting does not affect game wins/losses, daily/hourly rewards, or robberies - only direct player-to-player transfers.

docs.title: /transactions
docs.text: View your recent transactions - payments sent, payments received, deposits, withdrawals, and other money events.

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Shows your transaction history with sortable views and pagination. Free users see a recent slice; Patreon supporters get full history plus extra sort modes that let you see who's paid you the most or who you've paid the most.

docs.title: Optional Fields
docs.text:
• sort - How to order results. Options:
    • Newest First (default)
    • Oldest First
    • Highest Amount First
    • Lowest Amount First
    • Who Sent Me Most Money (Patron only)
    • Who I Sent Most Money To (Patron only)
  • per_page - Transactions to show per page. Range: 5–15 (default 10).

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /transactions [sort] [per_page]

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /transactions /transactions sort:"Highest Amount First" /transactions sort:"Newest First" per_page:15

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 5 seconds.
  • The two "top sender / top recipient" sort modes are Patreon-only and aggregate totals across your entire history.
  • All money events are recorded: deposits, withdrawals, /pay transfers, game wins/losses, etc.
  • Use /transactions_toggle to block incoming payments entirely.

docs.title: /withdraw
docs.text: Move money from your bank back to your wallet so you can spend, gamble, or send it.

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Withdraws the requested amount from your bank into your wallet. You'll need wallet balance to gamble in any casino game, send via /pay, or buy from most shops. Note that money in your wallet can be robbed, so only withdraw what you need.

docs.title: Required Fields
docs.text:
• amount - How much to withdraw. Accepts plain numbers (5000), shorthand (5k, 2.5m, 1b), or keywords:
    • max - Withdraw your entire bank balance.
    • half - Withdraw half of it.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /withdraw [amount]

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /withdraw 10000 /withdraw 1.5m /withdraw max

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 3 seconds.
  • Minimum withdrawal: $100.
  • Capped at your current bank balance.
  • Every withdrawal is recorded in /transactions.

docs.title: Robbery
docs.text: Steal directly from other players - but lose big if you're caught. The robbery system also includes bounties (put a price on someone's head) and anonymous mode (hide your identity at a tax cost).

docs.title: How robbing works
docs.text:
When you target another player with /rob user:

  • Stake = 50% of the target's wallet balance. You need that much in your own wallet just to attempt the robbery.
  • Success chance: 35%.
  • On success: you take the stake (minus 2% lottery tax, minus 15% extra if you're in anonymous mode).
  • On failure: you lose the stake amount as a fine, plus the lottery tax.
  • Either way: both you and the target are payment-blocked for 30 minutes - neither of you can use /pay during that window.

Targets must be in the same Discord server as you, and you must have at least $1,000 in your wallet to attempt any rob.

docs.title: Bounty system
docs.text: You can put a money bounty on another player's head with /rob bounty set. Anyone who successfully robs (or even fails to rob) the target claims the bounty. Bounties are per-server and have an expiry timer (max 7 days). The setter can remove their own bounty for a refund or top it up later.

docs.title: Anonymous mode
docs.text: Toggle with /rob anonymous. When enabled, victims see "Anonymous Robber 🎭" instead of your name in the rob notification. Costs an extra 15% tax on profits (paid to the lottery pool on top of the standard 2%).

docs.title: Commands
docs.text:
All robbery actions are subcommands of /rob:

Subcommand What it does /rob user Attempt to rob another player /rob anonymous Toggle anonymous mode (15% tax) /rob stats View robbery history /rob bounty set Place a bounty on someone /rob bounty list See all active bounties in this server /rob bounty info Check if a specific user has a bounty /rob bounty remove Cancel a bounty you set (refund) /rob bounty increase Top up an existing bounty

docs.title: Cooldowns and protection
docs.text:
• /rob user cooldown: 30 minutes (Patreon T3+: ~19.5 min). Voting halves this further (50% reduction).
  • 30-minute payment block on both attacker and victim after every rob attempt - see /pay for what's blocked.
  • Money in your bank is safe from robbery. Only wallet balance can be stolen - keep it banked when you're not playing.

docs.title: /rob
docs.text: Steal money from other players, manage anonymous mode, view your stats, and run the bounty system.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
/rob is a command group with eight subcommands covering all of the robbery system. The headline action is /rob user - everything else supports it.

See the Robbery overview for the full mechanics; this page covers each subcommand's exact arguments and behavior.

docs.title: /rob user
docs.text:
Attempt to rob another player.

Usage: /rob user [target]

Required Fields:

  • target - The Discord member to rob. Must be in the same server as you.

Mechanics:

  • Stake: 50% of target's wallet balance. You must have that much in your own wallet to attempt.
  • Success chance: 35%.
  • Minimum balance to attempt: $1,000 in your wallet.
  • On success: you receive (stake − 2% tax − 15% anonymous tax if enabled). Target loses the full stake.
  • On failure: you lose the stake (capped at your wallet) as a fine. Target keeps their money.
  • Both outcomes: 30-minute payment block applied to both players (no /pay either direction during that window).

Cooldown: 30 minutes (Patreon T3+: ~19.5 min). /vote rewards halve this for one cycle.

docs.title: /rob anonymous
docs.text:
Toggle whether your identity is hidden from rob victims.

Usage: /rob anonymous

Behavior: Toggles between on and off.

  • When enabled: victims see "Anonymous Robber 🎭" instead of your name. Costs an extra 15% tax on robbery profits (paid to lottery pool).
  • When disabled: standard 2% tax only; victims see your name.

Cooldown: 5 seconds.

docs.title: /rob stats
docs.text:
View robbery statistics for yourself or another player.

Usage: /rob stats [user]

Optional Fields:

  • user - Mention another player to view their stats. Defaults to you.

Shows: total attempts, successful robberies, failed robberies, success rate, money won, money lost (failed attempts), money lost (as victim), net profit/loss, average win, average loss, anonymous mode status.

Cooldown: 5 seconds.

docs.title: Bounty system
docs.text: Subcommands under /rob bounty for placing and managing bounties on other players. Bounties are per-server - a bounty in Server A doesn't carry to Server B.

docs.title: /rob bounty set
docs.text:
Place a money bounty on another player.

Usage: /rob bounty set [target] [amount] [expiry]

Required Fields:

  • target - Player to put the bounty on. Cannot be yourself or a bot.
  • amount - Money amount. Accepts numbers, shorthand (k/m/b/t), or min/max/half. Minimum $1,000.
  • expiry - How long the bounty lasts. Format: 1d, 48h, 30m. Min 1 minute, max 7 days.

The amount is deducted from your wallet immediately. Whoever robs (or attempts to rob) the target claims the bounty automatically - even on failed rob attempts.

Cooldown: 10 seconds.

docs.title: /rob bounty list
docs.text:
Browse all active bounties in this server.

Usage: /rob bounty list

Paginated view with target name, amount, time remaining, and bounty setter.

Cooldown: 5 seconds.

docs.title: /rob bounty info
docs.text:
Check if a specific player has a bounty on them.

Usage: /rob bounty info [user]

Optional Fields:

  • user - Player to check. Defaults to you.

Cooldown: 5 seconds.

docs.title: /rob bounty remove
docs.text:
Cancel a bounty you set and get refunded.

Usage: /rob bounty remove [target]

Required Fields:

  • target - The player whose bounty (yours, on them) to cancel.

You can only remove bounties you set yourself.

Cooldown: 10 seconds.

docs.title: /rob bounty increase
docs.text:
Add more money to a bounty you've already placed.

Usage: /rob bounty increase [target] [amount]

Required Fields:

  • target - The player whose bounty to top up.
  • amount - Additional money. Accepts shorthand and shortcuts.

Cooldown: 10 seconds.

docs.title: General notes
docs.text:
• All robberies require same-server targets.
  • Bank balances are safe - only wallet money can be stolen.
  • The 30-minute payment block applies regardless of robbery outcome and blocks both directions of /pay.
  • /vote halves your /rob user cooldown for the next cycle.
  • 2% of every rob (success or fail) is taxed into the lottery pool, plus 15% extra in anonymous mode.

docs.title: Scratch Tickets
docs.text: Scratch cards are reward tickets you earn from voting (and weekly claims for donators). Open them with /scratch open to win money, farm boosts, lottery tickets, jackpots, or even Patreon gift memberships.

docs.title: How you get cards
docs.text:
Source Cards Vote for the bot (/vote) 1 card per vote Weekly claim (/scratch weekly-scratch) 10 cards/week (donator) - 15/week for tier 8 (GODLY) Gifted from another player (/scratch pay) Variable

Money rewards scale with your base vote reward - the higher your vote streak, the bigger every money-prize multiplier becomes.

docs.title: Command list
docs.text: Command Description /scratch open Open one or more cards /scratch inventory View your stored non-money rewards /scratch history Paginated history of all scratch sessions /scratch stats View totals + best win for any user /scratch pay Gift unopened cards to another user /scratch pay-history View cards sent and received /scratch weekly-scratch Donator-only free weekly cards

docs.title: Rarity tiers
docs.text:
Every card draws one reward weighted by rarity:

Rarity Combined chance What you can win Common ~65% 0.5×–1.25× vote money, small farm boost (60–75 min), 1 lottery ticket Uncommon ~22% 1.5×–2.5× money, 90–105 min farm boost, 2 lottery tickets, DONATOR Patreon gift (very rare) Rare ~10% 3×–4.5× money, 120–150 min farm boost, 3 lottery tickets, DONATOR/CHILL gifts Epic ~2.8% 6×–8× money, 10× jackpot, 180–210 min farm boost, 5 lottery tickets, CHILL/EPIC/SUPER/MEGA gifts Legendary ~0.2% 15× jackpot, 25× MEGA jackpot, 240–360 min farm boost, 10 lottery tickets, SUPER → GODLY gifts

docs.title: Reward types
docs.text:
• Money - instant money, calculated as base_vote_reward × multiplier.
  • Farm Boost - reduces crop grow time by 60–360 minutes (3 hours = 180 min epic). Stored if no crops planted.
  • Lottery Ticket - automatically added to the current lottery draw (1, 2, 3, 5, or 10 tickets).
  • Jackpot - large money prize (10× or 15× vote reward).
  • Mega Jackpot - 25× vote reward (legendary only).
  • Patreon Gift - 7-day membership at the rolled tier (DONATOR → GODLY). Stored in inventory; recipient must claim manually.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Each card opens one reward.
  • Money rewards apply instantly. Non-money rewards (farm boosts, Patreon gifts) can be used now or stored in your inventory for later.
  • Patreon gifts always go to the inventory and must be claimed.
  • See /vote to earn cards.

docs.title: /scratch history
docs.text: Paginated history of every scratch session - what you opened, what you won, and when.

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Each time you open one or more cards in a session, a record is saved. This command lets you flip through those past sessions (most recent first) and see exactly which rewards came out.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /scratch history

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /scratch history

docs.title: What it shows
docs.text:
The history embed paginates 5 sessions per page with arrow buttons to navigate. Each session shows:

  • Date opened.
  • Number of cards in that session.
  • Rewards received (money, boosts, gifts, lottery tickets).
  • Total money won that session.

A summary panel on every page shows your overall stats:

  • Total Sessions - how many times you've scratched.
  • Total Cards Opened - lifetime cards across all sessions.
  • Total Winnings - lifetime money earned from scratches.
  • Best Single Win - your single best scratch payout.
  • Patreon Gifts Won (if any) - total gifts pulled.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 5 seconds.
  • 5 sessions per page; pagination buttons appear if you have more.
  • For just the totals (no per-session breakdown), see /scratch stats.
  • For gift-transfer history (cards sent/received), see /scratch pay-history.

docs.title: /scratch inventory
docs.text: View and manage stored scratch card rewards - Patreon gifts and farm boosts you saved for later instead of using immediately.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
When you open a scratch card and choose Store instead of Use Now, the reward goes here. From the inventory you can:

  • Apply farm boosts to your current crops on demand.
  • Claim Patreon gifts (activates a 7-day membership at the rolled tier).
  • Remove items you don't want.

Money rewards never appear here - they're applied instantly when scratched.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /scratch inventory

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /scratch inventory

docs.title: What's stored
docs.text:
Item Source Action Farm Boost Stored from a scratch open Apply to currently growing crops to cut grow time Patreon Gift Always stored on draw Claim to activate a 7-day membership at the rolled tier Lottery Tickets (auto-applied at scratch time) -

Patreon gifts are stored automatically because activating one replaces your current membership tier for 7 days - you decide when to use it.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 3 seconds.
  • Farm boosts only apply if you have crops planted; the bot tells you which crops were boosted.
  • Patreon gifts give you the rolled tier's perks (vote bonuses, weekly scratches, multipliers) for 7 days from claim.
  • See /scratch open for how rewards land here, and the Scratch Tickets index for the full reward list.

docs.title: /scratch open
docs.text: Open your scratch cards and reveal the rewards. Each card draws one weighted reward from the prize pool.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
If you have any unopened cards, this command pulls up the scratch interface - pick how many to open at once and the bot animates the scratch and reveals each reward. Money rewards apply instantly; farm boosts and Patreon gifts can be used now or stored in your inventory.

You earn cards by voting (/vote - 1 card per vote) or, if you're a donator, by claiming /scratch weekly-scratch.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /scratch open

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /scratch open

docs.title: Reward pool
docs.text:
Money prizes are calculated from your base vote reward, so a higher vote streak = bigger payouts.

  • Money - 0.5× to 8× the vote reward (50%–800%)
  • Jackpots - 10× / 15× / 25× mega jackpot (legendary)
  • Farm Boosts - 60–360 minutes off grow time
  • Lottery Tickets - auto-entered into the current lottery draw
  • Patreon Gifts - 7-day memberships (DONATOR through GODLY)

See the Scratch Tickets index for the full rarity breakdown.

docs.title: Choosing what to do with non-money rewards
docs.text:
When you draw a farm boost or Patreon gift, you'll get a prompt:

  • Use Now - apply immediately (boosts your currently growing crops, etc.).
  • Store - save it to your inventory and claim later via /scratch inventory.

Patreon gifts always go to inventory and must be claimed manually - they aren't applied automatically because they replace your active membership tier for 7 days.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 3 seconds.
  • No cards = the command shows you how to get some (/vote).
  • Money rewards apply instantly to your wallet.
  • Farm boosts can only be applied if you have crops planted; otherwise they're stored.
  • See /scratch history for past sessions and /scratch stats for totals.

docs.title: /scratch pay-history
docs.text: View every scratch card you've sent or received via /scratch pay.

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Paginated log of all gift transfers - both inbound (cards you got from friends) and outbound (cards you sent away). Each entry shows the other user, the amount, the reason (if one was set), and the timestamp.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /scratch pay-history

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /scratch pay-history

docs.title: What it shows
docs.text:
The history embed paginates 10 transfers per page with navigation buttons. Each transfer line includes:

  • Direction - sent or received.
  • Other user (mention).
  • Amount of cards.
  • Reason, if the sender provided one.
  • When the transfer happened.

Top-of-embed totals:

  • Total Sent - lifetime cards you've gifted out.
  • Total Received - lifetime cards you've received.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 5 seconds.
  • 10 entries per page.
  • Up to 100 transfers shown total.
  • For session-level scratch history (what you opened), see /scratch history.
  • For sending cards, see /scratch pay.

docs.title: /scratch pay
docs.text: Gift unopened scratch cards from your account to another user.

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Transfer cards from your inventory to a friend. After running the command you'll see a confirmation prompt - if you confirm, the cards leave your account and arrive on theirs immediately. The recipient gets a notification and can open the cards with /scratch open.

docs.title: Required Fields
docs.text:
• user - Discord member to send cards to.
  • amount - Number of cards to gift (must be ≥ 1).

docs.title: Optional Fields
docs.text: • reason - Free-text note shown on the confirmation and saved in transfer history.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /scratch pay [user] [amount] [reason]

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /scratch pay @username 5 /scratch pay @username 10 reason:"happy birthday" /scratch pay @username 1

docs.title: Restrictions
docs.text:
• Can't gift to yourself.
  • Can't gift to bots.
  • Can't gift more cards than you own - the bot rejects the command if you don't have amount.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 3 seconds (with an additional internal 5-minute gift cooldown to prevent abuse).
  • Confirmation prompt times out after a short window - confirm or it cancels.
  • This action cannot be undone once confirmed.
  • See /scratch pay-history to view what you've sent and received.
  • See /scratch open for how the recipient redeems them.

docs.title: /scratch stats
docs.text: Quick scratch card stats for yourself or another user - totals, best win, current card count, average win.

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Lighter than /scratch history - this just shows the totals, not the per-session breakdown. Great for checking how a friend is doing or for a quick "where am I at?" snapshot.

docs.title: Optional Fields
docs.text: • user - Discord member to look up. Defaults to yourself.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /scratch stats [user]

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /scratch stats /scratch stats @username

docs.title: What it shows
docs.text: Field What it is Cards Available Unopened cards on the user's account Total Opened Lifetime cards opened Total Winnings Lifetime money won from scratches Best Win Single biggest scratch payout ever Patreon Gifts Won Lifetime Patreon gift drops (if any) Last Activity Relative timestamp of their last scratch Average Win total_winnings ÷ total_opened

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 5 seconds.
  • Public - anyone can look up anyone's stats.
  • For the per-session breakdown of your own openings, see /scratch history.
  • For gift transfers in/out, see /scratch pay-history.

docs.title: /scratch weekly-scratch
docs.text: Donators-only free weekly scratch card claim. Claim once per week to add 10 (or 15) cards to your account.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Active donators (any Patreon tier) can claim a weekly batch of free scratch cards. The cards drop straight into your account - open them with /scratch open whenever you want.

This is one of the perks of supporting Paradise Bot on Patreon.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /scratch weekly-scratch

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /scratch weekly-scratch

docs.title: Cards per claim
docs.text: Donator tier Cards per week Tier 1 – 7 (DONATOR through CRAZY) 10 Tier 8 (GODLY) 15 Non-donator ❌ Locked

docs.title: Reset timing
docs.text:
• The week resets on a fixed schedule - the embed shows the next reset timestamp when you've already claimed.
  • After reset, the claim is available again.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 5 seconds (slash-command cooldown - the weekly limit is enforced separately).
  • Donators only. Non-donators see a Patreon prompt instead of a claim.
  • Cards stack with anything you already have - they don't replace, they add.
  • See the Scratch Tickets index for what cards can win.
  • For the donator membership itself, see the Patreon shop.

docs.title: Frequently Asked Questions
docs.text:
Quick answers to the most common questions about Paradise Bot.

  • Can I customize the bot?

    Language, prefix, and per-server settings

  • Emojis aren't working

    Why emojis may not show and how to fix it

  • Restrict commands to channels

    Limit commands to specific channels or roles

  • Terms of Service

  • Why are commands restricted?

    Cooldowns, locks, and access requirements

docs.title: Can I customize the bot?
docs.text: Short answer: Limited customization for server admins, but most settings are global.

docs.title: Server-level (admin permissions required)
docs.text:
• Channel restrictions - limit which channels Paradise Bot responds in. See Restrict commands to channels?.
  • Disable specific commands - admins can selectively disable commands per server through the bot's admin UI.
  • Bot prefix - Paradise Bot is slash-commands only, so there's no prefix to customize.
  • Roles - assign Patreon-tier-style cosmetic roles via integrations.

docs.title: User-level
docs.text:
• TOS acceptance (one-time, required) - see /tos.
  • Toggle incoming payments - see /transactions_toggle.
  • Pet name - rename your pet (2-week cooldown). See /pets.
  • Patreon role flair - comes automatically with subscription tiers.

docs.title: What you can't customize
docs.text:
• The actual game mechanics, payouts, multipliers, or cooldowns are global and cannot be changed per-server.
  • Random number generation is server-side and not configurable.
  • Currencies are bot-wide (one shared "$" balance across all servers you use the bot in).

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• Restrict commands to channels
  • Why are commands restricted?

docs.title: Emojis aren't working
docs.text: Short answer: Paradise Bot uses custom emojis from a private Discord server. If the bot can't access them, they show up as broken :emoji_name: text or blank squares.

docs.title: Why it happens
docs.text:
Paradise Bot's embeds use custom Discord emojis hosted in a private guild. Custom emojis only render correctly when:

  1. The user (you) is in a server where Discord can see them, or
  2. The bot has permission to use external emojis in your channel.

If neither is true, the emoji shows as raw text like <:bag_of_money:1375759016759922720> or just appears blank.

docs.title: As a user
docs.text:
• Make sure you're a member of the Paradise Bot support server so Discord recognizes the emojis. Use /links to get the invite.
  • Update your Discord client - older versions sometimes can't render newer custom emojis.

docs.title: As a server admin
docs.text:
Grant the bot the Use External Emojis permission in your server / channel:

  1. Server Settings → Roles → Paradise Bot's role.
  2. Enable Use External Emojis and Use External Stickers.
  3. Also check channel-level permission overrides.

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• /links - invite to support server
  • Why are commands restricted?

docs.title: Can I disable or restrict commands to certain channels?
docs.text: Short answer: Yes - Paradise Bot supports per-channel and per-command restrictions for server admins.

docs.title: How it works
docs.text: Server admins can restrict Paradise Bot to specific channels using Discord's built-in channel permissions plus the bot's admin commands.

docs.title: Discord-level (no bot help needed)
docs.text: Paradise Bot only responds where it has the Send Messages, Use Slash Commands, and Embed Links permissions. Remove those in the channel's permission overrides and the bot becomes silent there.

docs.title: Recommended setup
docs.text:
• Create a dedicated #bot-spam or #casino channel.
  • Lock the bot to that channel via channel permissions.
  • Keep the rest of your server clean.

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• Why are commands restricted? - for users hitting "this command is restricted" errors.
  • Can I customize the bot?

docs.title: Why is the bot saying all commands are restricted?
docs.text: Short answer: The server admin has restricted Paradise Bot to a specific channel - try moving to that channel.

docs.title: 1. Channel restriction (most common)
docs.text:
The server admin set up channel restrictions through the bot or Discord permissions. Paradise Bot responds in dedicated channels (e.g., #casino or #bot-spam) but ignores you everywhere else.

Fix: Find your server's bot channel and run commands there. Ask a moderator if you're not sure which channel.

docs.title: 2. Missing permissions for the bot
docs.text:
The bot doesn't have Send Messages, Use Slash Commands, or Embed Links in your current channel.

Fix (admin): Grant the bot those permissions in the channel's permission overrides.

docs.title: 3. You haven't accepted TOS
docs.text:
Most commands require TOS acceptance. If you've never accepted, you'll get a TOS prompt that may look like a restriction.

Fix: Run /tos and click Accept.

docs.title: 4. You're banned from the bot
docs.text:
If a moderator/admin has banned your account from Paradise Bot, every command will refuse.

Fix: Open a support ticket via /links - Discord support server.

docs.title: 5. Maintenance mode
docs.text:
The bot owner has put Paradise Bot into maintenance mode for an update. Most commands return a maintenance message.

Fix: Wait - usually a few minutes. Check the support server for status updates.

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• Can I customize the bot?
  • Restrict commands to channels?
  • /tos

docs.title: Is the bot rigged?
docs.text: No. Every outcome is determined by random number generation. The bot has no memory of past results and doesn't factor in your balance when deciding outcomes.

docs.title: How the RNG works
docs.text:
Not all games use the same method — and that's intentional. Python's built-in random module is fast but not cryptographically secure; it's based on a predictable algorithm that can technically exhibit patterns over billions of draws. For everyday game logic that's fine, but for high-stakes draws like the lottery, we go further.

The lottery winner selection uses multiple stacked layers of entropy:

  • secrets module — pulls from OS-level randomness (e.g. /dev/urandom on Linux), which is cryptographically secure and genuinely unpredictable
  • SHA-256 hash mixing — blends ticket data, timestamps, and random bytes into a hash that drives additional shuffling
  • Microsecond timing entropy — injects real-time clock precision as an extra unpredictable variable
  • Multiple independent shuffle passes — the pool is shuffled several times using different sources before winners are selected

The result is that for anything involving large prize draws, the randomness is as close to true randomness as software can get.

docs.title: House edge
docs.text: Some games do have a house edge, and that's completely normal. It's the same reason real casinos can offer big payouts and still keep running. It's not there to cheat you — it's just what makes the economy sustainable and the games fair over time. You can absolutely win, and people do.

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• /stats - Track your win/loss record per game
  • /transactions - Full history of every bet

docs.title: What is the Terms of Service?
docs.text: Short answer: A required agreement covering acceptable use, ban policies, and refund/dispute rules. Run /tos and click Accept once to use the bot.

docs.title: Why it exists
docs.text:
Paradise Bot is a simulated gambling bot. The TOS clarifies:

  • No real money is at stake. All "money" is fictitious in-game currency.
  • Patreon donations are not refundable. They support the bot's development; they don't entitle you to specific game outcomes.
  • Bans for cheating, exploit abuse, or harassment are at the developer's discretion.
  • The bot is provided as-is - no warranty, no guarantee of uptime.
  • Privacy - what data the bot stores (user IDs, balances, transaction logs) and how long.

docs.title: Accepting

docs.title: Banking System
docs.text: Store your money safely and earn daily interest.

docs.title: How Banking Works
docs.text: Your bank account is separate from your wallet and keeps your money safe from robberies. You earn interest daily on money in your bank.

docs.title: Safety Features
docs.text:
• Protected from robbing - Money in your bank cannot be stolen by other players
  • Daily interest - Earn money automatically every day at 11:00 UTC

docs.title: Interest Rates
docs.text:
Interest is calculated based on your bank balance:

  • Up to $5B: 10% daily interest
  • $5B - $50B: 9% daily interest
  • $50B - $500B: 8% daily interest
  • $500B - $2T: 7% daily interest
  • $2T - $10T: 6% daily interest
  • $10T - $50T: 5% daily interest
  • $50T+: 4% daily interest

Maximum daily interest: $15 Trillion per day

docs.title: Bank Tax
docs.text:
Large bank accounts may be taxed periodically: - Tax rate: 5% of bank balance - Purpose: Funds the community lottery system

Inactive users (2+ days without activity) face a 2% tax on wallet balance.

docs.title: Bank Vault Sizes
docs.text:
You can upgrade your bank vault capacity through /shop:

Vault Tier Capacity Price Basic Bank Account $1B Free (default) Bronze Vault $5B $500M Silver Vault $25B $2.5B Gold Vault $100B $12.5B Platinum Vault $500B $75B Diamond Vault $2T $400B Emerald Vault $5T $1.25T Ruby Vault $10T $3T Sapphire Vault $20T $7.5T Obsidian Fortress $35T $15T Celestial Treasury $50T $25T

docs.title: Basic Banking
docs.text:
• /deposit <amount> - Put money in your bank (minimum $100)
  • /withdraw <amount> - Take money out of your bank (minimum $100)

docs.title: Special Keywords
docs.text:
• max - Deposit/withdraw maximum possible amount
  • half - Deposit/withdraw half of available money
  • k/m/b suffixes supported (e.g., "100m" = $100,000,000)

docs.title: Other Commands
docs.text:

  • /transactions - View your banking history

docs.title: Tips
docs.text:
1. Keep money in bank - Earn interest and stay safe from robberies
  2. Upgrade your vault - Bigger vaults let you store more money
  3. Check daily - Interest is paid at 11:00 UTC every day
  4. Plan ahead - If your bank is full, interest goes to your wallet instead

docs.title: Voting & Rewards
docs.text: Paradise Bot's voting system provides daily rewards for supporting the bot on listing websites, with streak bonuses and special benefits.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Vote for Paradise Bot on various listing sites to earn money, build voting streaks, and unlock exclusive bonuses that benefit your entire bot experience.

docs.title: Voting Benefits
docs.text:
• Daily Rewards - Earn millions of dollars
  • Streak Bonuses - Exponentially increasing rewards
  • Farming Acceleration - Crops grow 1.5 hours faster
  • Cooldown Reduction - Rob command cooldown halved
  • Heist Bonuses - Special multipliers for heist participation
  • Scratch Cards - Bonus lottery tickets for voters

docs.title: /vote Command
docs.text: Opens the comprehensive voting interface with: - Real-time Status - See which sites are available - Direct Links - One-click access to voting pages - Reward Preview - Next reward amount display - Streak Progress - Current and highest streak tracking

docs.title: Available Voting Sites

  • Discord Bot List (DBL) - Secondary listing platform

Each site has a 12-hour cooldown between votes.

docs.title: Base Rewards
docs.text: Vote rewards start at a base amount and increase significantly with streak bonuses: - Days 1-5: Base reward + 20% per day - Days 6-10: Base + 100% + 30% per additional day - Days 11-15: Base + 250% + 50% per additional day - Days 16-20: Base + 500% + 70% per additional day - Days 21-30: Base + 850% + 100% per additional day - Day 31+: Maximum reward tier reached

docs.title: Patron Bonuses

docs.title: Building Streaks

  • Streak Maintenance - Miss a day and streak resets to 0
  • Maximum Streak - Personal best streak tracking

docs.title: Streak Milestones
docs.text: Day 7: Farming Unlock - Vote bonus applies to crop growth Day 10: Property Protection - Reduced property taxes Day 15: Enhanced Rewards - Significant multiplier boost Day 20: Cooldown Master - All cooldowns reduced Day 25: VIP Status - Exclusive features unlocked Day 30: Legend Tier - Maximum benefits achieved

docs.title: Crop Acceleration
docs.text: Each vote accelerates ALL your growing crops by 1.5 hours: - Instant Benefit - Applied immediately upon voting - All Crops - Affects every planted slot - Stack with Fertilizer - Combines with other growth bonuses - No Limit - Works regardless of crop type or growth stage

docs.title: Farming Slot Bonuses
docs.text:
Voting Streaks Unlock Additional Farming Slots: - Day 10: +1 extra farming slot (permanent while streak maintained) - Day 20: +2 total extra slots - Day 30: +3 total extra slots (maximum bonus)

Losing your streak removes these bonus slots!

docs.title: Rob Cooldown Reduction
docs.text: Each vote halves your current rob command cooldown: - Active Cooldown Required - Only works if rob is on cooldown - 50% Reduction - Cuts remaining time in half - Instant Application - Reduction applied immediately - Strategic Timing - Vote right after using rob for maximum benefit

docs.title: Heist Bonuses
docs.text: Voting provides special bonuses for heist participation: - Damage Multipliers - Increased damage output - Success Rate Bonus - Higher chance of heist success - Loot Bonuses - Additional rewards from completed heists - Duration: 12 hours from last vote

docs.title: Bonus Scratch Cards
docs.text: Every vote grants one free scratch card: - Automatic Reward - Added to inventory immediately - Use /scratch - Open cards for random prizes - Valuable Prizes - Can contain millions in rewards - Stack Unlimited - Collect multiple cards

docs.title: Major Milestones
docs.text: Reach specific streak milestones for one-time bonuses: - Day 7: Special achievement badge - Day 14: Large money bonus - Day 30: Exclusive title unlock - Day 50: Mega reward package - Day 100: Legendary status and perks

docs.title: Farming System
docs.text: Plant crops, wait for them to grow on real-world timers, and harvest for a profit. Farming is Paradise Bot's main passive income system - buy seeds with money you already have, walk away, come back hours later, sell the produce for more than you paid.

docs.title: How it works
docs.text:
1. Run /farming to open your farm.
  2. Click Plant to pick a crop, choose how many to plant, optionally apply fertilizer, and pick which slot(s).
  3. Wait - every crop has its own real-time timer (27 hours for wheat up to 57 hours for diamond melon).
  4. Once ready, click Harvest to collect your money.
  5. Optionally Replant to refill the same slots with the same crops + fertilizers in one click.

docs.title: Crops
docs.text:
11 crop tiers, each with a base price, sell price, and grow time. Profit margins range from 3% (corn) to 8% (watermelon, diamond melon):

Tier Crop Buy Sell Grow time Profit Beginner Wheat $5M $5.2M 27 h ~4% Corn $20M $20.6M 29 h ~3% Carrot $10M $10.35M 28 h ~3.5% Mid-tier Potato $15M $15.75M 31 h ~5% Tomato $40M $42.4M 32 h ~6% Pumpkin $150M $159M 34 h ~6% High-tier Grapes $1.2B $1.272B 38 h ~6% Watermelon $5B $5.4B 41 h ~8% Premium Golden Apple $20B $20.8B 49 h ~4% Diamond Melon $100B $108B 57 h ~8%

Profits look small per crop - but you can plant up to 100 crops per slot, and slots run in parallel.

docs.title: Fertilizers
docs.text:
Optional speed-up purchased per planting:

Fertilizer Cost / crop Speed boost Basic $10M 20% faster (0.8× grow time) Super $100M 40% faster (0.6×) Mega $2B 60% faster (0.4×)

Fertilizer cost is per-crop, paid up front when planting.

docs.title: Patreon perks
docs.text:
Donator tiers stack a profit boost and a grow-time speed boost on every crop:

Tier Name Profit boost Grow speed Tier 1 DONATOR +15% +10% Tier 2 CHILL +25% +15% Tier 3 EPIC +30% +20% Tier 4 SUPER +50% +25% Tier 5 MEGA +65% +30% Tier 6 ULTIMATE +80% +35% Tier 7 CRAZY +100% (2×) +40% Tier 8 GODLY +200% (3× profit) 3× faster (33% grow time)

Donators (T1+) also unlock auto-replant in farm settings - your crops auto-harvest and replant on a small profit tax.

docs.title: Random events
docs.text:
A daily/randomly-firing event system can buff or debuff currently-planted crops:

Effect What happens Speed boost Crops finish X hours sooner Speed penalty Crops take X hours longer Profit boost Increases sell price by % Profit penalty Decreases sell price by %

Events only affect crops that were already planted when they trigger - empty fields = no impact. Use /farming-events to see recent events and whether they hit your crops.

docs.title: Commands
docs.text:
• /farming - Open your farm interface
  • /farming-events - Recent events and your impact
  • /farming-stats - Detailed farming stats and totals
  • Scratch farm boosts - Free time reductions from scratch cards

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• 100 crops per slot maximum.
  • Crops only spoil if you ignore them indefinitely; harvest when ready.
  • Very large farms (1000+ crops) get a longer harvest timeout to prevent timeouts.
  • Use /farm-cleanup (20s cooldown) if your farm gets stuck or laggy.
  • Scratch card farm boosts can shave 60–360 minutes off your current crops' grow time - see /scratch open.

docs.title: /farming-events
docs.text: View the last few farming events that fired - and whether they affected your crops.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Random crop events trigger throughout the day and either buff or debuff all crops that were already planted at the time. This command shows you the 5 most recent events with timestamps, what they did, how many farmers and crops they hit, and a green check / red X for whether your own crops were affected.

If you had no crops planted when an event fired, you weren't affected - events only apply to crops that were already in the ground.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /farming-events

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /farming-events

docs.title: What it shows
docs.text:
For each of the last 5 events:

  • Event name + emoji (e.g. 🎃 Harvest Festival)
  • How long ago it triggered
  • Effect - speed boost (Xh faster), speed penalty (Xh slower), profit boost (X% more), or profit penalty (X% less)
  • Reach - number of crops affected and number of farmers hit
  • Your status - ✓ your crops were affected, or ✗ you had no crops growing

Plus a summary line:

  • Your Events Summary: 3/5 recent events affected your crops
  • Whether you currently have crops growing right now

docs.title: Event types
docs.text:
Effect type What it does Speed boost Cuts grow time by X hours Speed penalty Adds X hours to grow time Profit boost Adds X% to sell price Profit penalty Subtracts X% from sell price

Rarities range from common buffs to legendary jackpot events like Harvest Festival (massive profit boost across all farmers).

docs.title: How to benefit
docs.text: Plant crops and leave them growing. Events only help if you have something in the ground when they fire - empty slots get nothing, even if a legendary event hits. Plant strategically and check this command to see if your timing paid off.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Public command - anyone can run it.
  • Shows only the last 5 events the bot has fired.
  • Events fire automatically; admins can manually trigger via /crop-event (admin-only).
  • For full farm management, see /farming. For totals, see /farming-stats.

docs.title: /farm-view
docs.text:
Admin-only command for viewing detailed information about another user's farm.

Admin-only command

/farm-view is restricted to the Paradise Bot admin server. Regular users cannot run it.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Diagnostic command used by bot administrators to inspect a specific user's farm - slot contents, crop timers, fertilizers, and any anomalies. Mostly used for support tickets and debugging stuck crops.

If you're not an admin, this command won't appear or will refuse to run.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /farm-view [user]

docs.title: Required Fields (admin only)
docs.text: • user - Discord member whose farm to inspect.

docs.title: What admins see
docs.text:
• Every slot's current crop, quantity, fertilizer, and planted_at timestamp.
  • Calculated grow-time vs. real elapsed time (for spotting stuck crops).
  • Any Patreon adjustments currently applied.
  • DB shard the user's farm lives on (farms are sharded across 10 SQLite DBs for performance).

docs.title: For regular users
docs.text: If you want to see your own farm, use /farming - that's the public entry point.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Admin server only. Slash command is registered with guild_ids=[ADMIN_SERVER_ID].
  • For ad-hoc support, ping admins instead of running this directly.
  • Related admin commands: /farm-debug, /farm-fix-stuck-times, /farm-cleanup, /farm-reset.

docs.title: /farming
docs.text: Open your farm interface - plant new crops, harvest ready ones, browse seeds, and manage settings.

docs.title: Description
docs.text: The main entry point for the entire farming system. Running /farming brings up an interactive embed with buttons for every action: Plant, Harvest, Replant, Settings, and a live view of your slots and growing crops.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /farming

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /farming

docs.title: Buttons available
docs.text: Button What it does Plant Pick a crop, quantity, fertilizer, and slot(s). Pays the seed cost up front. Harvest Collect every ready crop in one click. Money lands instantly. Replant After a harvest, replant the same crops + fertilizers in the same slots in one click. Shop Browse seeds and fertilizers. Settings Toggle auto-replant (donators), notifications, etc.

docs.title: Plot rules
docs.text:
• Each slot holds up to 100 crops.
  • Slots run in parallel - planting in slot 1 doesn't block slot 2.
  • Mixed slots are fine: each slot stores one crop type at a time, but different slots can hold different crops.

docs.title: Performance notes
docs.text:
For very large farms, harvest takes longer:

Total crops Harvest timeout Normal 12 seconds Large 18 seconds Very large 25 seconds

If a harvest times out (only happens with massive farms during peak load), retry - your crops aren't lost. Use /farm-cleanup (20s cooldown) for stubborn issues.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 6 seconds.
  • The Plant menu shows base grow time - fertilizer and Patreon speed boosts apply on top once you confirm.
  • Auto-replant (donator perk) charges a small tax on profit per harvest cycle as the cost of the convenience.
  • Scratch card farm boosts apply to currently-growing crops only - see /scratch open.
  • For full crop/fertilizer/Patreon tables, see the Farming index.
  • For event impact, see /farming-events. For totals, see /farming-stats.

docs.title: /farming-stats
docs.text: Detailed lifetime farming statistics - totals planted, harvested, money earned, favorite crop, and performance metrics.

docs.title: Description
docs.text: The numbers-on-a-page view of your farming history. Shows everything from how many crops you've ever planted to your total fertilizer spend.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /farming-stats

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /farming-stats

docs.title: What it shows
docs.text:
The stats embed surfaces lifetime numbers across all your slots and shards:

  • Crops Planted (lifetime) - total seeds you've put in the ground.
  • Crops Harvested - total successful harvests.
  • Total Earned - gross sell-price money from all harvests.
  • Total Spent on Seeds - what you've paid out for seed costs.
  • Total Spent on Fertilizer - fertilizer outlay.
  • Net Profit - earned minus seed and fertilizer spend.
  • Favorite Crop - most-planted crop type.
  • Most Used Fertilizer - basic, super, mega, or none.
  • Patreon perks active (if any) - current profit/speed boost from your tier.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 10 seconds.
  • Public - covers your own stats only.
  • Auto-replant cycles count as normal harvests for stat purposes.
  • For per-event impact, see /farming-events. For the full crop catalog, see the Farming index.

docs.title: Fishing
docs.text: Cast a line at one of 13 locations, catch fish ranging from Common to Mythical, and level up your rod for bigger payouts. Fishing is the passive grind counterpart to mining and pets - same earning potential, different gameplay rhythm.

docs.title: Core loop
docs.text:
1. /fish casts your line at your current location.
  2. The bot rolls a fish from that location's pool, weighted by rarity.
  3. Sell value goes straight to your wallet; XP applies toward level-up.
  4. Level up to unlock new locations and stronger rods.
  5. Higher rod tier = more money, more XP, faster cooldown, better rare-fish chances.

docs.title: Commands
docs.text:
Command What it does /fish Cast a line - main fishing action /fish-stats Lifetime fishing stats and biggest catches

The fishing shop (rod purchases) is built into the /fish interface - no separate command.

docs.title: Fish rarities
docs.text:
Catch rate is rolled per cast:

Rarity Base catch rate Color ⚪ Common 60% Light grey 🟢 Uncommon 25% Green 🔵 Rare 10% Blue 🟣 Epic 3.9% Purple 🟡 Legendary 1% Gold 🔴 Mythical 0.1% Red

Rod rare-fish bonus multiplies legendary/mythical chances.

docs.title: Locations (13 total)
docs.text: Location Level Description 🌿 Beginner's Pond 1 Tiny Fish, Bass, Catfish, Golden Carp 💧 Mountain Creek 5 Brook Trout, Rainbow Fish, Crystal Fish 🌊 Rushing River 10 Salmon, Sturgeon, River Dragon 🏖️ Mystic Lake 15 Lake Bass, Giant Carp, Lake Monster, Ancient Fish 🥬 Murky Swamp 25 Mudfish, Swamp Eel, Bog Beast, Swamp Spirit 🌊 Deep Ocean 35 Tuna, Swordfish, Great White, Giant Squid 🐠 Coral Reef 45 Tropical Fish, Reef Guardian, Ancient Turtle, Coral Spirit 🌌 The Abyss 60 Anglerfish, Abyssal Horror, Leviathan, Void Beast ✨ Void Realm 75 Void Fish, Reality Bender, Cosmic Horror 🌟 Celestial Waters 100 Star Swimmer (Epic), Reality Bender (Legendary) 🔬 Quantum Stream 125 High-level cosmic fish 🌋 Primordial Depths 150 Ancient creation-era beasts 🌠 Cosmic Nexus 200 Endgame

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: base 4 seconds (slash command), modified down by rod cooldown reduction.
  • One equipped rod at a time; switch via the /fish rod menu.
  • Fishing events fire occasionally and buff catch rates / payouts.
  • Equal-payout alternative: Pets. Different pacing - fishing is more passive, pets need active care.

docs.title: /fish-stats
docs.text: Detailed lifetime fishing statistics - catches, earnings, level, biggest catch, location breakdown.

docs.title: Description
docs.text: The numbers-on-a-page view of your fishing career. Shows everything tracked across every cast you've ever made.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /fish-stats

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /fish-stats

docs.title: What it shows
docs.text:
• Level + XP - current level and progress toward the next.
  • Equipped rod - your active rod with all its bonuses.
  • Total Catches - lifetime fish caught.
  • Total Earnings - lifetime money from selling fish.
  • Current streak / Longest streak - consecutive successful casts (no events disrupt streaks).
  • Last catch time - relative timestamp of your most recent catch.
