docs.title: /baccarat
docs.text: Classic casino card game - bet on Player, Banker, or Tie and watch the closer-to-9 hand win.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
You place a single bet, then choose one of three outcomes. The bot deals two hands (Player and Banker) following standard baccarat third-card rules, and pays out based on which hand wins.

Card values: 2–9 = face value, 10/J/Q/K = 0, A = 1. Hand totals over 9 use only the second digit (e.g., 15 = 5). Closest to 9 wins.

docs.title: Required Fields
docs.text: • bet - Amount to wager. Accepts numbers, shorthand (k, m, b, t), or shortcuts (min, max, half).

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /baccarat [bet]

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /baccarat 100k /baccarat 5m /baccarat max

docs.title: Payouts
docs.text:
Bet Multiplier Profit on win Player 2.0× 1:1 (your bet doubled) Banker 1.95× 1:1 minus 5% commission Tie 9.0× 8:1

The 5% banker commission is the standard real-casino edge. Tie pays 8:1 but rarely hits.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 5 seconds.
  • One game at a time - finish or let the lobby expire before starting another.
  • The lobby auto-closes after 60 seconds if no bet is placed; your money is returned.
  • Recent results history is shown after each round (last 10 outcomes).
  • The bot follows standard baccarat third-card rules - neither player nor banker can be hit-or-stand independently.

docs.title: /blackjack
docs.text: Classic blackjack vs the dealer - hit, stand, double down, or split, and try to beat the house without busting.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
You're dealt two cards face-up; the dealer gets one face-up and one face-down. Standard blackjack rules apply: get as close to 21 as possible without going over. Aces count as 1 or 11 (whichever is better), face cards = 10.

The dealer peeks for blackjack on Ace or 10-value showing, hits soft 17, and stands on hard 17+. Single-deck game.

docs.title: Required Fields
docs.text: • bet - Amount to wager. Accepts shorthand (k, m, b, t) or shortcuts (min, max, half).

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /blackjack [bet]

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /blackjack 100k /blackjack 1m /blackjack half

docs.title: Player actions
docs.text: Action When available Effect Hit Always Take another card Stand Always End your turn Double Down Any 2-card hand (incl. after split) Double your bet, take exactly one card Split Two cards of equal value Split into two hands (max 4 splits)

docs.title: Payouts
docs.text:
Outcome Payout Blackjack (21 with 2 cards) 3:2 (1.5× profit) Win vs dealer 1:1 Push (tie) Bet returned Loss / bust Bet lost

If both you and the dealer have blackjack on the deal, it pushes.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 2 seconds (the fastest casino game).
  • Max splits: 4 (so up to 4 separate hands).
  • Double after split: allowed.
  • Dealer rules: hits soft 17, stands on hard 17+, peeks for blackjack on Ace/10.
  • 60-second turn timer - if you don't act in time, you auto-stand on the current hand.
  • Game errors automatically refund your bet.
  • Strategy matters: blackjack rewards skilled play more than any other casino game.

docs.title: /coinflip
docs.text: Pick heads or tails - fair 50/50, double or nothing.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
You place a bet, the bot reserves the result, and you pick HEADS or TAILS from a button. The coin flips with a brief animation. Match the result and you double your money; miss and you lose your bet.

This is the cleanest game in the casino - no bonus rounds, no scaling multipliers, no tricks. Just a coin.

docs.title: Required Fields
docs.text: • bet - Amount to wager. Accepts shorthand (k, m, b, t) or shortcuts (min, max, half).

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /coinflip [bet]

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /coinflip 100k /coinflip 5m /coinflip max

docs.title: Payout
docs.text: Outcome Result Win 2× your bet (1:1 profit) Loss Bet lost

docs.title: Limits
docs.text:
• Min bet: $10,000
  • Max bet: $50 trillion (/coinflip overrides the standard $1T limit)

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 4 seconds.
  • 30-second decision timer - if you don't pick within 30 seconds the bet is refunded.
  • The result is locked in before you pick - choosing faster doesn't change anything.
  • Game has the lowest possible variance per spin: it's literally always 2× or 0.
  • For player-vs-player coinflips, see /coinflip-duel instead.

docs.title: /crash
docs.text: Watch a multiplier climb in real time - cash out before it crashes.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
You place a bet and a multiplier starts at 1.00× and climbs. At some random moment the multiplier "crashes" and the game ends. If you've already cashed out, you keep bet × multiplier_at_cashout. If you haven't, you lose the bet.

The crash point is determined the moment the round starts (you can't see it). The minimum cashout multiplier is around 1.05×–1.35× (varies per round).

docs.title: Required Fields
docs.text: • bet - Amount to wager.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /crash [bet]

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /crash 100k /crash 5m /crash max

docs.title: How it works
docs.text:
1. Bet is deducted on round start.
  2. Multiplier begins climbing - slow at first, faster as it grows.
  3. The CASH OUT button is disabled briefly at the start (1–3s window).
  4. Once enabled, click it any time to lock in bet × current multiplier.
  5. If the multiplier crashes before you click, you lose the bet.

docs.title: Crash zones (rough distribution)
docs.text:
The bot heavily weights the crash point near the early-cashout zone:

Range Approximate frequency 1.01× – 1.20× ~15% (instant crash) 1.20× – 1.50× ~55% (the danger zone) 1.50× – 2.00× ~15% 2.00× – 3.00× ~10% 3.00× – 5.00× ~5%

So most rounds resolve below 1.5× - high multipliers exist but are rare.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 5 seconds.
  • Minimum cashout: ~1.05× (game-dependent - sometimes higher for repeat early-cashout players).
  • Cashout window opens 0.1–3.5 seconds after the round starts; you can't cash out before the button activates.
  • Frequent low-multiplier cashouts can shift future rounds against you (the game tracks behavior).
  • Game errors auto-refund your bet.
  • Pure-luck game - no strategy beats the underlying randomness, but cashing out at lower multipliers loses less per round on average.

docs.title: /dice
docs.text: Pick a number 1–6, place a bet, and roll a single die. Match the number for a 3× payout.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
You pick a number from 1 to 6 and bet on it. The bot rolls a six-sided die. If your number comes up, you win 3× your bet (2× profit). Otherwise the bet is lost.

The actual hit rate is around 29% - slightly above the natural 16.7% for a single die, but below the 33% break-even point for a 3× payout, which gives the house its edge.

docs.title: Required Fields
docs.text:
• bet - Amount to wager.
  • number - The number you're betting on (1–6).

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /dice [bet] [number]

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /dice 100k 6 /dice 5m 3 /dice max 1

docs.title: Payout
docs.text: Outcome Result Match 3× bet (2× profit) Miss Bet lost

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 4 seconds.
  • The bet number is forced from the dropdown - no typos possible.
  • A short rolling animation plays before the result is shown.
  • Compare with /diceduel for a player-vs-player version.

docs.title: /horse-race
docs.text: Pick one of 5 horses, place a bet, and watch a live animated race. Better-odds horses pay less; underdogs can pay up to 6× your bet.

docs.title: Description
docs.text: You pick horse #1 through #5 and a bet amount. The race plays out with a live progress bar - horses advance with semi-random speeds based on their base odds, stamina, and chaos events. First past the finish line wins.

docs.title: Required Fields
docs.text:
• bet - Amount to wager.
  • horse - Horse number to bet on (1–5).

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /horse-race [bet] [horse]

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /horse-race 100k 1 /horse-race 5m 5 /horse-race max 3

docs.title: The horses
docs.text:
# Name Emoji Odds (multiplier) 1 Thunder 🐎 2.5× (favorite) 2 Storm 🏇 3.2× 3 Flash 🐴 4.0× 4 Star 🦄 4.8× 5 Wind 🐪 6.0× (longshot)

A $100K bet on Wind (#5) pays $600K if it wins.

docs.title: Race mechanics
docs.text:
• Track length: 20 squares.
  • Race duration: 10–16 seconds (random).
  • Per-step movement: base speed × random 0.5–1.9 × stamina × chaos events.
  • Big events (~25% per step): can wildly speed up or slow a horse.
  • Chaos events (~18% per step): another random multiplier on top.
  • Final sprint triggers if no winner by the timer - boosts every horse with high random variance.
  • Ties are broken with a random offset, slightly favoring the underdog.

So while the favorite has a real edge, upsets are common.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 5 seconds.
  • Pre-race embed shows the lineup, your horse, and potential winnings before the race starts.
  • Hit Start Race to begin (if you walk away, the lobby cleans up after 60s).
  • Winnings are credited at the end with a full results breakdown.

docs.title: /minesweeper
docs.text: Reveal safe tiles on a 4×5 grid hiding 4 mines. Each safe tile pumps your multiplier - cash out before you click a mine.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
You bet, and the bot generates a 4 row × 5 column grid (20 tiles total) with 4 mines hidden among them - leaving 16 safe tiles. Click any tile:

  • Safe → multiplier increases by +0.20×
  • Mine → instant loss, game over

Reveal all 16 safe tiles and you auto-cash-out for the maximum payout. Or click Cash Out at any time once your multiplier is high enough.

docs.title: Required Fields
docs.text: • bet - Amount to wager.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /minesweeper [bet]

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /minesweeper 100k /minesweeper 5m /minesweeper max

docs.title: Multiplier table
docs.text:
Starting multiplier is 0.80× (cashing out before any reveals would actually lose money). Each safe tile adds 0.20×:

Tiles revealed Multiplier 0 0.80× (locked) 1 1.00× (locked) 2 1.20× (locked) 3 1.40× (locked) 4 1.60× ← cashout unlocks here 5 1.80× 6 2.00× 8 2.40× 10 2.80× 12 3.20× 14 3.60× 16 (perfect) 4.00× (auto-cashout)

docs.title: Mine probability
docs.text:
Per click, with m mines remaining and t tiles unrevealed: chance of hitting a mine = m / t. So the danger ramps up as the grid empties.

Tiles revealed Approximate mine risk on next click 0 4/20 = 20% 4 4/16 = 25% 8 4/12 = 33% 12 4/8 = 50% 14 4/6 = 67%

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 7 seconds.
  • Cashout requires multiplier ≥ 1.6× - minimum 4 safe tiles revealed.
  • 180-second game timer - running out forfeits the bet.
  • Hitting a mine reveals all other mines on the board.
  • Pure-luck game - there's no minesweeper-style number hint; every tile is equally likely.

docs.title: /plinko
docs.text: Buy plinko balls at a fixed cost each, drop them all at once, and let each ball roll into a multiplier slot. High variance - most balls lose, a rare few hit massive multipliers.

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Plinko uses a fixed cost per ball instead of a free-form bet. You pick how many balls to buy (1–10000), then click DROP BALLS to resolve them all at once. Each ball lands on a multiplier between 0.1× (big loss) and 100× (jackpot).

docs.title: Required Fields
docs.text: • balls - Number of balls to buy (1 to 10000).

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /plinko [balls]

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /plinko 1 /plinko 100 /plinko 1000

docs.title: Pricing
docs.text:
• Cost per ball: $1,000 (fixed)
  • Total cost: 1000 × balls

So /plinko 1000 costs $1M up front.

docs.title: Multiplier table
docs.text:
Each ball independently rolls one of these multipliers:

Multiplier Approx. probability Tier 0.1× 35% Big loss 0.3× 28% Medium loss 0.5× 20% Small loss 0.8× 10% Minor loss 1.2× 5% Small win 2.0× 1.5% Medium win 5.0× 0.4% Good win 10.0× 0.08% Great win 25.0× 0.03% Huge win 50.0× 0.01% Massive win 100.0× 0.005% Jackpot

So about 93% of balls return less than your cost, but the heavy upside compensates over many drops.

docs.title: Streak adjustments
docs.text:
The game tracks recent results across drops:

  • 3 losses in a row boost a tiny chance (~10%) of a small comeback ball.
  • Any recent win sharply raises (~90%) the chance the next ball loses.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 4 seconds.
  • Drop is one click - all balls resolve simultaneously, you can't cash out mid-drop.
  • Net profit/loss is shown along with a per-multiplier breakdown.
  • Massive announcements only trigger when net profit is large.
  • For a single ball expected return: roughly $700–$800 per $1,000 spent (heavy variance, dominated by jackpots).

docs.title: /poker
docs.text: 6-card draw poker against the dealer. You're dealt 6 cards, choose which to discard, then the bot picks your best 5-card hand and compares it to the dealer's.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Place a bet. You receive 6 cards. Pick which (if any) to discard - they're replaced from the deck. Then the dealer reveals their 6 cards. The best 5-card poker hand from each side is compared; the higher rank wins.

Hand strength matters more than just winning - your payout multiplier scales with the rank of your hand.

docs.title: Required Fields
docs.text: • bet - Amount to wager. Accepts shorthand or shortcuts.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /poker [bet]

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /poker 100k /poker 5m /poker max

docs.title: Discard options
docs.text:
After the deal, you have three buttons:

  • Keep All Cards - Stand pat with your initial 6.
  • Discard All Cards - Replace all 6 cards.
  • Select Cards - Pick exactly which cards to discard (any combination).

docs.title: Hand multipliers
docs.text:
Multipliers are paid on win (in addition to your bet returned):

Hand Multiplier Notes Royal Flush 40× The jackpot Straight Flush 20× Four of a Kind 6.5× Full House 4× Flush 2.5× Straight 2× Three of a Kind 1.5× Two Pair 1.1× Slight profit Pair 0.85× Slight loss even on win High Card 0.5× Half your bet back

So a Royal Flush on a $100K bet pays $4M profit ($4.1M total return).

docs.title: Tie / loss
docs.text:
• Tie (same rank and tiebreakers): bet returned.
  • Dealer wins: bet lost.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 3 seconds.
  • Turn timer: 120 seconds to finalize your discards. Auto-resolves with all cards kept on timeout.
  • Dealer advantage: ~30% chance the dealer is given a slightly favored hand for the comparison.
  • Single deck - your discards and dealer's hand are dealt from the same shuffled remainder.
  • "Play Again" button appears after each hand for fast re-rolls at the same bet.
  • Game errors auto-refund the bet.

docs.title: /roulette
docs.text: American roulette - bet on color, number, range, dozens, or basket. The wheel has 0, 00, and 1–36.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
You place a bet, then choose one of 14 betting options from the buttons (or pick a custom number 0–36). The wheel spins with an animation, and a number is drawn.

Greens (0 and 00) get a slightly elevated draw rate (~3% each instead of the usual ~2.7%) to give the house its edge.

docs.title: Required Fields
docs.text: • bet - Amount to wager (placed when you run the command).

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /roulette [bet]

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /roulette 100k /roulette 5m /roulette max

docs.title: Bet types & multipliers
docs.text:
Bet Coverage Multiplier Red 18 numbers 1.75× Black 18 numbers 1.75× Even 1–36 even 1.75× Odd 1–36 odd 1.75× 1–18 Low half 1.75× 19–36 High half 1.75× 1st Dozen 1–12 1.95× 2nd Dozen 13–24 1.95× 3rd Dozen 25–36 1.95× Basket 0, 1, 2, 3 5.75× Green (0) Single 10× Double zero (00) Single 10× Choose Number Single 0–36 10×

(Note: the Choose Number modal accepts 0–36, not 00.)

docs.title: Wheel layout
docs.text:
• Red numbers: 1, 3, 5, 7, 9, 10, 12, 14, 16, 18, 19, 21, 23, 25, 27, 32, 34, 36
  • Black numbers: the rest of 1–36
  • Green: 0 and 00

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 3 seconds.
  • One bet per spin - pick your option, animation plays, result is shown.
  • 180-second game timer - if you don't bet in time the lobby cleans up.
  • 0 and 00 are the only ways the house wins on Red/Black/Even/Odd/range bets.
  • Best long-term odds: outside bets (Red/Black/Even/Odd/halves) at 1.75×. Worst: single number / green at 10× (true odds would be ~37×).

docs.title: /rps
docs.text: Rock Paper Scissors against the bot - best of 3 rounds, double-or-bet-back.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
You play three rounds of RPS against the bot. Whoever wins more rounds takes the match.

If the match ends tied (e.g., 1-1-1), your bet is returned - no money lost.

docs.title: Required Fields
docs.text: • bet - Amount to wager.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /rps [bet]

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /rps 100k /rps 5m /rps max

docs.title: Match outcomes
docs.text: Result Payout You win (more rounds won than bot) 2× bet (1× profit) Tie (equal round wins) Bet returned You lose Bet lost

docs.title: Round mechanics
docs.text:
Per round, the bot's pick is weighted (against your pick):

Bot picks Probability The move that beats you 35% Same as you (tie) 32.5% The move that loses to you 32.5%

So you win ~32.5% of rounds, tie ~32.5%, lose ~35%. Over three rounds, you're slightly favored to lose the match.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 4 seconds.
  • 180-second game timer - if you take too long the bet is returned.
  • Each round shows the result with a history of previous rounds.
  • For player-vs-player RPS see /rps-multiplayer.

docs.title: /slots
docs.text: Classic 3-reel slots - match three symbols across the line for multiplier payouts up to 50×.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
You bet, the reels spin, and three symbols are drawn. Match all three for a payout based on the symbol; mismatches lose the bet.

The game tracks your win/loss streaks, total spin count, and time since last play to apply mercy and re-engagement bonuses.

docs.title: Required Fields
docs.text: • bet - Amount to wager.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /slots [bet]

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /slots 100k /slots 5m /slots max

docs.title: Symbol payouts
docs.text:
Symbol Multiplier 7️⃣ Seven 50× (jackpot) 🎯 Bar 25× 🍉 Watermelon 15× 💎 Diamond 8× 🍌 Banana 4× 🍇 Plum 2.5× 🍒 Cherry 1.5×

A 50× hit on a $100K bet pays $5M. Cherry is the most common winning symbol.

docs.title: Win mechanics
docs.text:
• Base win rate: ~12% per spin (varies with player history).
  • New player bonus: +5% win rate for the first 15 spins.
  • First win guarantee: 7th spin auto-wins on Cherry if you've never won.
  • Mercy win: triggers after 10 losses in a row (small Cherry/Plum win).
  • Welcome back: auto-win after 4+ days away from the game.
  • Cooldown trigger: after 3 wins in a row, the next spin is forced to lose to dampen runaway streaks.
  • Veteran tax: symbol probabilities slightly worsen after 300 spins.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 4 seconds.
  • A short spinning animation plays before the result.
  • Wins ≥ 10× your bet trigger a "BIG WIN" embed; ≥ 20× broadcasts to the channel.
  • VIP/Premium/Donor server roles get a +10% bonus on any win.
  • Stats are tracked per-player (total spins, wagered, won, streaks) and used to balance future spins.

docs.title: /sportsbet
docs.text: Place real-money bets on live sports games using real-time odds from The Odds API. Bets resolve automatically when the game ends.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Run /sportsbet to open the Sports Betting Hub. Pick a sport, browse upcoming games, and place a bet on a team - the multiplier (odds) shown is the real-world bookmaker payout. When the game finishes, the bot checks the result and credits or debits your account automatically.

This is the most "real casino" game in the bot - no internal RNG, just live data.

docs.title: Required Fields
docs.text: None - the command opens an interactive selector. Pick your sport and game from menus.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /sportsbet

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
/sportsbet

(Run the command, then use the dropdowns and buttons to navigate.)

docs.title: Available sports
docs.text:
Category Leagues American Football NFL, College Football (NCAAF) Basketball NBA, WNBA, College (NCAAB) Baseball MLB, KBO Ice Hockey NHL Soccer Premier League, Champions League, La Liga, Bundesliga, Ligue 1, Serie A, MLS Combat MMA, Boxing Tennis ATP Wimbledon / US Open / French Open Rugby / Aussie Rules NRL, AFL Cricket IPL

Availability depends on the season - only games with active markets show up.

docs.title: How odds work
docs.text:
Odds are displayed as decimal multipliers:

  • Team A 2.50 vs Team B 1.80 means $100 on Team A pays $250 on win, $100 on B pays $180 on win.
  • Lower number = more likely to win.
  • The implicit margin is the bookmaker's edge (typically 4–10%).

docs.title: Limits
docs.text:
• Min bet: $100
  • Max bet: $30 trillion per single bet
  • Max active bets: 15 pending bets per user (counted on games that haven't started yet or are within ~4 hours of start)

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 10 seconds (longest of any casino game - anti-spam).
  • Bets are stored in a separate database with full audit logging.
  • A background task automatically checks completed games and credits winnings without you needing to do anything. Payouts typically land within a few hours of the game ending.
  • Live odds come from The Odds API and are cached for up to ~3 hours, so the odds you see may lag the bookmakers slightly. The odds shown when you place your bet are the odds you get.
  • If the system is down, you'll see "Sports Betting Unavailable" - this usually clears within a few minutes.
  • Internal rate limiter prevents bet spam; you'll see a warning if you hit it.

docs.title: /tower
docs.text: Stack blocks one at a time - each block adds to the multiplier, but a failed block topples the tower and you lose the bet.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
You bet, and the bot picks one of 8 tower types (cosmetic - affects stability and multiplier slightly). Each click of Add Block rolls against a success chance that drops as the tower grows taller. Successful blocks raise your multiplier; a failed block ends the run for $0.

You can Cash Out at any time after the first block to lock in bet × current multiplier.

docs.title: Required Fields
docs.text: • bet - Amount to wager.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /tower [bet]

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /tower 100k /tower 5m /tower max

docs.title: Tower types (random)
docs.text:
Each game picks one tower type. Higher stability = better success per block, higher multiplier boost = bigger payouts:

Tower Stability Multi boost 🪨 Stone 1.10 0.90× <:Star1:1341147145410969712> Divine 1.05 1.00× 🏆 Golden 1.00 1.10× <:Diamond:1375484760071340062> Crystal 0.95 1.05× 🌑 Shadow 0.90 1.15× 👁️ Eldritch 0.88 1.18× 🔮 Glass 0.85 1.20× ☠️ Corrupted 0.80 1.25×

So Corrupted Tower is the highest-reward, lowest-success build.

docs.title: Mechanics
docs.text:
• Base success starts around 85% and drops by ~2.5% per block (modified by tower stability).
  • The tower has a hidden unstable threshold at height 5–8. Past that, success drops faster and stability decays each block.
  • Tiny critical failure chance (~1% per block past height 4) - even a high-success roll can collapse.
  • Multiplier growth is roughly +0.10–0.13 per successful block, scaled by tower type.
  • Cashout has a small collapse risk (capped at 10%) - extremely rare but possible at extreme heights.
  • Cashout payout uses random ±5% variance plus a possible cashout event: lucky bonus, treasure find, market fee, or tower tax.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 3 seconds.
  • Cash Out requires at least 1 successful block.
  • 180-second game timer.
  • Pure-luck game - there's no skill in clicking; just deciding when to stop.
  • Higher towers = exponentially higher reward but exponentially higher collapse chance. Most profitable strategy is cashing out around heights 4–6.

docs.title: /wheel-of-fortune
docs.text: Spin an 8-segment wheel for outcomes from total loss to a progressive jackpot. The jackpot grows from every player's bet until someone hits it.

docs.title: Description
docs.text: You bet, the wheel spins through a four-frame animation, and the pointer settles on one of 8 segments. Each segment has a fixed weight (probability) and payout multiplier. The Jackpot segment pays the entire current jackpot pool - which scales with your bet and the global pool.

docs.title: Required Fields
docs.text: • bet - Amount to wager.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /wheel-of-fortune [bet]

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /wheel-of-fortune 100k /wheel-of-fortune 5m /wheel-of-fortune max

docs.title: Wheel segments
docs.text:
Segment Multiplier Approx. weight <:bag_of_money:1375759016759922720> Jackpot 30–50× (dynamic) ~0.01% 🌟 Super Win 12× ~0.18% 💎 Big Win 5× ~0.95% ✨ Small Win 2× ~6.8% 🎲 Mini Win 1.2× ~11.5% 🔄 Breakeven 1× (bet returned) ~14% 📉 Small Loss 0.4× (40% back) ~31% 💔 Loss 0× (full loss) ~35.6%

So ~67% of spins lose money, ~14% break even, and ~19% win something.

docs.title: Jackpot pool
docs.text:
• Starts at $10,000.
  • Each bet contributes 1% of the bet to the pool.
  • The Jackpot multiplier scales with bet size: between 30× and 50×.
  • When hit, the pool resets to $10,000 and the win is announced server-wide.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 4 seconds.
  • Win/loss streak tracker shows hot/cold messaging after 2+ wins or losses.
  • Use /wheel-stats (run /wheel-stats directly) to see your spins, win rate, ROI, and best streak.
  • Bigger bets → bigger jackpot multiplier → larger absolute jackpot payouts.

docs.title: Heists
docs.text: Big-money cooperative jobs (and a faster solo variant). Pick a location, set the entry fee, recruit a crew, and roll the dice on a payout multiplier.

docs.title: How heists work
docs.text:
Two modes:

  • /heist - Multiplayer. Host a job in a channel, players join with the same entry fee, the host picks a location, then the bot rolls success and splits the pot.
  • /solo-heist - Solo. Three-phase one-player heist. Higher base success (77%), smaller payout multiplier (1.5×–1.8×), shorter cooldown.

Both modes count toward the same daily heist limit and share the same cooldown timer.

docs.title: Daily limits
docs.text:
Every player has a daily cap on how many heists they can run (across multiplayer + solo combined):

Tier Daily limit Non-Patreon 4 Donator / Chill / Epic (T1–T3) 6 Super / Mega (T4–T5) 8 Ultimate / Crazy (T6–T7) 10 Godly (T8) 15

Vote bonus: voting on both Top.gg and DBL within 24 hours adds +2 daily heists, taking a free user up to 6 and Godly to 17.

docs.title: Tax on winnings
docs.text:
A portion of every winning heist is taxed and goes to the community lottery pool:

Tier Tax rate Non-Patreon 20% Donator / Chill / Epic (T1–T3) 15% Super / Mega (T4–T5) 5% Ultimate / Crazy / Godly (T6–T8) 0%

docs.title: Cooldowns
docs.text:
• Multiplayer heist: 10-minute cooldown after the heist resolves (win or lose).
  • Solo heist: 15 minutes on win, 10 minutes on loss.
  • The two share a single timer - running a multiplayer heist locks out solo and vice versa.

docs.title: Locations
docs.text: Multiplayer heists pick from 8 locations. Each has its own player range, base success rate, and reward multiplier. See /heist for the full table.

docs.title: Commands
docs.text:
• /heist - Host a multiplayer heist
  • /solo-heist - Run a heist alone
  • /heist-stats - Your heist history (wins, losses, profit, daily progress)

docs.title: The Weekly Major Heist
docs.text: Once a week (Saturdays), the Weekly Major Heist runs as a community event - up to 150 players, 78% base success rate, 4.0× payout multiplier. Watch your server's announcement channel.

docs.title: /heist-stats
docs.text: Your full heist record - wins, losses, profit, win rate, daily progress, and favorite locations.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Shows lifetime heist statistics for yourself or another player: total heists hosted vs joined, total wins and losses, money won, money lost, net profit, win rate, favorite location, and total participants you've led on heists you hosted.

Also shows your daily heist progress - current count vs your daily limit (with vote bonus included), so you can tell at a glance how many runs you have left today.

docs.title: Optional Fields
docs.text: • user - Mention another player to view their heist stats. Defaults to you.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /heist-stats [user]

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /heist-stats /heist-stats @username

docs.title: What it shows
docs.text:
• Heists Joined / Hosted - counts split by your role
  • Wins / Losses / Win Rate
  • Money Won / Money Lost / Net Profit - color-coded gold (profit), red (loss), blue (break-even)
  • Favorite Location - most-frequently-targeted heist location
  • Total Participants Led - how many other players have been on heists you hosted
  • Daily Progress - X / Y heists today where Y includes vote bonus
  • Patreon tier badge if applicable

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 5 seconds.
  • Stats include both multiplayer and solo heists. Solo heists are logged under location solo.
  • Daily progress resets at midnight UTC.
  • Players who haven't run a heist yet show a friendly empty-state message instead of zeros.
  • Vote bonus (+2 daily heists) is included in the displayed daily limit when active.

docs.title: /heist
docs.text: Host a multiplayer heist in the current channel. Set the entry fee, recruit a crew, pick a location, and split the payout.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Starts a heist lobby in the channel where you ran the command. Other players can join by paying the same entry fee. Once you have enough players, you (the host) pick a location from a dropdown - different locations require different crew sizes and have different success rates and reward multipliers.

When the heist runs, the bot rolls success against the location's base rate. On a win, the pot is multiplied by the location multiplier and split among participants (minus tax). On a loss, everyone loses their entry fee.

Only one heist per channel can be active at a time. You can't host or join a heist if you're already in one (multiplayer or solo).

docs.title: Required Fields
docs.text: • entry_fee - The amount each player must pay to join. Accepts numbers, shorthand (k, m), or shortcuts (min, max, half).

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /heist [entry_fee]

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /heist 100k /heist 5m /heist max

docs.title: Locations
docs.text: Location Players Base success Reward multi Difficulty Local Bank 2–4 77% 1.8× Easy Luxury Mansion 2–3 75% 1.9× Easy Lucky Casino 3–5 68% 2.3× Medium Royal Train 3–4 62% 2.3× Hard Federal Vault 4–6 57% 2.1× Hard Space Taco Research Facility 3–12 75% 3.4× Medium Capital Reserve 10–50 68% 3.5× Very Hard Paradise Vault 2–15 80% 3.2× Patreon Exclusive

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 10 minutes after the heist resolves (win or lose). Shared with /solo-heist.
  • Daily limit: counts toward your shared heist limit (4 for non-Patreon, up to 17 with Patreon + voting). See the Heists overview.
  • Tax on winnings: 20% non-Patreon, 0% for Ultimate (T6) and above. See the overview.
  • Entry fee is deducted when you host. If the heist fails to start (errors, no joiners), you're refunded.
  • The location dropdown shows player count requirements and difficulty for each option. Patreon-exclusive locations are locked for free users.
  • Once joined, players can't leave - entry fee is committed.
  • Use /heist-stats to track your win rate and daily progress.

docs.title: /solo-heist
docs.text: Run a heist on your own - higher base success rate than multiplayer, smaller payout multiplier, no need to wait for a crew.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
A three-phase solo heist (Infiltration → Vault Access → Escape) at a randomly-chosen location. Each phase rolls independently with success chance starting at 77% and dropping by 3% per phase as you go deeper. You can fail one phase and still succeed; fail two and the heist is busted.

On success: payout = entry_fee × (1.5 to 1.8 random multiplier), minus tax. On failure: you lose your entire entry fee.

Solo heists count toward the same daily limit as multiplayer heists, but resolve faster (no recruiting, no joinable lobby) so they're the better option when you want quick action.

docs.title: Required Fields
docs.text: • entry_fee - Amount to risk. Accepts numbers, shorthand (k, m, b, t), or shortcuts (min, max, half).

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /solo-heist [entry_fee]

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /solo-heist 100k /solo-heist 5m /solo-heist max

docs.title: Locations
docs.text:
A location is chosen randomly per heist (cosmetic - doesn't change odds or payout):

  • Downtown Casino
  • Diamond Vault
  • Art Gallery
  • Luxury Penthouse
  • High-Security Bank

docs.title: Phase mechanics
docs.text:
Phase Base success Adjusted Infiltration 77% 77% Vault Access 77% 74% Escape 77% 71%

You can fail one phase and still complete the heist. Two failures and the job collapses.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Min entry: $10,000.
  • Max entry: $10 trillion.
  • Cooldown: 15 minutes on win, 10 minutes on loss. Shared with /heist (multiplayer).
  • Daily limit: counts toward the shared heist limit (4 for non-Patreon, up to 17 max). See overview.
  • Tax: same as multiplayer heist - 20% non-Patreon down to 0% Ultimate+.
  • Solo heists log to your stats under location "solo" - see /heist-stats.

docs.title: Multiplayer Games
docs.text: Player-vs-player and group games. Some are 1v1 duels, some are channel-wide free-for-alls. Most involve real money pots.

docs.title: Quick reference
docs.text: Command Type Players Cooldown /battleship 1v1 board 2 10s /connect4 1v1 board 2 3s /tic-tac-toe 1v1 board 2 4s /wordle duel 1v1 puzzle 2 5s /fight 1v1 turn-based combat 2 4s /coinflip-duel 1v1 chance 2 4s /diceduel 1v1 chance 2 4s /rps-multiplayer 1v1 RPS 2 4s /buckshot play 1v1 strategy 2 5s /simple-buckshot Group battle royale 2+ 4s /russian-roulette Group elimination 2+ 4s /multiplayer_dice Group elimination 2+ - /murdermystery Social deduction 4–15 5s /multiblackjack Multi-table casino 1–7 5s /heist & /solo-heist Group robbery 1–50 10m

docs.title: Common rules
docs.text:
• Bet shortcuts: All multiplayer games accept k/m/b/t shorthand and min/max/half shortcuts. Most have no max bet cap - you can bet your entire balance.
  • Min bet: $10,000 unless overridden.
  • Both players checked: the opponent's wallet must cover the bet too.
  • One game at a time: you can't be in two casino/multiplayer games simultaneously.
  • Bets are deducted up-front and the winner gets the full pot (or shared, in group games).
  • Challenge timeout: most 1v1 challenges expire after 30–60 seconds if the opponent doesn't accept.

docs.title: Skill games
docs.text: /battleship, /connect4, /tic-tac-toe, /wordle duel - pure mechanical skill, no luck.

docs.title: Combat
docs.text: /fight, /buckshot play, /simple-buckshot - turn-based with weapon/item choices.

docs.title: Pure chance
docs.text: /coinflip-duel, /diceduel, /russian-roulette - RNG decides.

docs.title: Mixed
docs.text: /rps-multiplayer, /multidice, /multiplayer_dice - some skill, mostly luck.

docs.title: Group
docs.text: /murdermystery, /multiblackjack, Heists - 3+ players in one lobby.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• For solo casino games, see the Casino Games index.
  • For multiplayer stats, use /stats with the appropriate game name.
  • Disputes/refunds: if a multiplayer game errors out, the bet is refunded automatically (or contact support if not - see Contact).

docs.title: /battleship
docs.text: ⚓ 1v1 Battleship - place ships on a hidden grid, take turns firing at coordinates, sink your opponent's whole fleet to win.

docs.title: Description
docs.text: You challenge another player. Both of you place ships on private boards (hidden from each other), then alternate firing at grid coordinates. Hits, misses, and sunk ships are tracked on a shared display. First to sink the enemy's entire fleet takes the pot.

docs.title: Required Fields
docs.text:
• opponent - Discord member to challenge.
  • bet - Amount each player puts up.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /battleship [opponent] [bet]

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /battleship @username 100k /battleship @username 5m

docs.title: Match flow
docs.text:
1. Challenge sent - opponent has limited time to accept.
  2. Both players place ships privately on their boards.
  3. Take turns calling shots (e.g., B5).
  4. Bot replies "Hit", "Miss", or "Sunk!" each turn.
  5. First to sink all opponent ships wins the pot (bet × 2).

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 10 seconds (longest in the multiplayer set - to discourage spam).
  • Min bet: $10,000.
  • No max bet cap - you can wager up to your wallet balance.
  • Both wallets must cover the bet to start.
  • Each player can only be in one battleship game at a time.
  • The board display includes a legend (your ships / your shots / opponent shots).
  • For other 1v1 board games, see /chess, /connect4, or /tic-tac-toe.

docs.title: /buckshot
docs.text: Buckshot Roulette - 1v1 shotgun standoff with random live/blank shells, items, and bluffing. Reduce your opponent to 0 HP to win the pot.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
/buckshot is a SlashCommandGroup with three subcommands:

Subcommand Description Cooldown /buckshot play [opponent] [entry_fee] Challenge another player 5s /buckshot stats [@user] View win/loss record + rank 3s /buckshot help Built-in rules embed 3s

Two players, one shotgun. Each round, the chamber is loaded with a random mix of 🔴 live and ⚫ blank shells. Counts are public; order is hidden. On your turn you choose to shoot the opponent or shoot yourself (if you blank yourself, you keep your turn). Items let you peek, skip, eject, or buff damage.

docs.title: /buckshot play
docs.text:
/buckshot play [opponent] [entry_fee]

  • opponent - Discord member to challenge.
  • entry_fee - Wager amount each player puts up.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /buckshot play @username 100k /buckshot play @username 5m /buckshot play @username max

docs.title: Match flow
docs.text:
1. Challenger runs the command; opponent has 60 seconds to accept.
  2. Both players locked in; pot = entry_fee × 2.
  3. Each round the chamber is loaded with a fresh mix of live/blank shells (counts shown).
  4. Players alternate turns (45 seconds per turn):
  5. Shoot Opponent - fires the next shell at them.
  6. Shoot Self - fires at you. Blank = no damage, keep turn. Live = take damage, turn passes.
  7. Use Item - burn a held item before shooting.
  8. First player to 0 HP loses the pot.

docs.title: Items (random per round)
docs.text:
• Magnifying Glass - peek at the next shell.
  • Cigarettes - restore 1 HP.
  • Beer - eject the next shell (skip it).
  • Saw - next shot deals double damage (live only).
  • Handcuffs - opponent skips their next turn.

(Exact item drops vary per round.)

docs.title: Stats & ranks (/buckshot stats)
docs.text:
Tracks: games played, won, lost, current streak, longest streak, win rate.

Rank tiers:

Rank Requirement Master 75%+ win rate, 10+ games Expert 60%+ win rate, 5+ games Skilled 45%+ win rate, 3+ games Rookie 1+ games Unranked 0 games

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Min bet: $10,000.
  • No max bet cap.
  • Each player can only be in one Buckshot game at a time.
  • The 45-second turn timer is strict - sit too long and your turn passes.
  • For other 1v1 nerve games, see /russian-roulette (group) or /fight (weapon-based).

docs.title: /coinflip-duel
docs.text: Pure 50/50 duel - both players pick a side of the coin (one heads, one tails), the bot flips, the correct picker takes the pot.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
You challenge another player. Both pick Heads or Tails - they must be different (the bot won't let you both pick the same side). The coin flips, and whoever picked the right side wins both bets.

Cleanest possible 1v1 in the bot - no skill, no comeback, just one flip.

docs.title: Required Fields
docs.text:
• opponent - Discord member to challenge.
  • bet - Amount each player puts up.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /coinflip-duel [opponent] [bet]

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /coinflip-duel @username 100k /coinflip-duel @username 5m /coinflip-duel @username max

docs.title: Match flow
docs.text:
1. Challenge sent - opponent has 60 seconds to accept.
  2. After accepting, both players pick Heads or Tails (must be different).
  3. Coin flips with a short animation.
  4. Winner takes bet × 2; loser loses the bet.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 4 seconds.
  • Min bet: $10,000.
  • No max bet cap - max confirms before locking in your full balance.
  • Both wallets must cover the bet to start.
  • Each player can only be in one game at a time.
  • For solo coinflip, see /coinflip.

docs.title: /connect4
docs.text: Classic 7-column Connect 4 against another player. First to line up four discs vertically, horizontally, or diagonally wins the pot.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Standard Connect 4 played in Discord. Each player drops a disc into one of 7 columns; gravity pulls it to the lowest empty row. First to make a line of 4 takes the bet.

You can play for fun (no money) by leaving the bet at 0, or stake real money against your opponent.

docs.title: Required Fields
docs.text: • opponent - Discord member to challenge.

docs.title: Optional Fields
docs.text: • bet - Amount to wager (defaults to 0 for a no-money game). Accepts k/m/b/t shorthand or min/max/half.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /connect4 [opponent] [bet]

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /connect4 @username - friendly match, no money /connect4 @username 100k /connect4 @username max

docs.title: Match flow
docs.text:
1. Challenge sent - opponent has 30 seconds to accept.
  2. Players alternate dropping discs via column buttons.
  3. First to line up 4 wins; full board with no winner = draw, bet refunded.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 3 seconds.
  • Min bet (if betting): $10,000.
  • No max bet cap - set bet: max to wager everything (a confirmation prompt appears).
  • Both players must have enough money to cover the bet.
  • Each player can only be in one game at a time.
  • For other free 1v1 board games, see /tic-tac-toe, /battleship, or /chess.

docs.title: /diceduel
docs.text: Best-of-3 dice rolls vs another player. Higher roll wins each round; first to 2 round wins takes the pot.

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Both players ante up the same bet. Each round, the bot rolls one die for each player and the higher number wins the round. First player to 2 round wins takes the entire pot. Ties on a single round don't count - neither player scores, the round repeats.

docs.title: Required Fields
docs.text:
• opponent - Discord member to challenge.
  • bet - Amount each player puts up.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /diceduel [opponent] [bet]

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /diceduel @username 100k /diceduel @username 5m /diceduel @username max

docs.title: Match flow
docs.text:
1. Challenge sent - opponent has 60 seconds to accept.
  2. Both players are locked into the duel; bet is held.
  3. Round 1: each player rolls a 6-sided die simultaneously.
  4. Higher roll wins the round; tie = no points.
  5. Repeat until one player wins 2 rounds.
  6. Winner takes bet × 2.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 4 seconds.
  • Min bet: $10,000 (uses standard bet_check).
  • No max bet cap - max wagers your entire balance.
  • Both wallets must cover the bet to start.
  • Each player can only be in one game at a time.
  • For solo dice, see /dice (single die guess) or /multidice (variable dice size + guess count).

docs.title: /fight
docs.text: Turn-based weapon duel - pick from 8 weapons, swing for damage, drop your opponent's HP to 0 to win the pot.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Both players start at 100 HP. Each picks one of 8 weapons (each with its own damage range, accuracy, and crit chance). Then you alternate turns - pick a move (every weapon has its own move list), the bot rolls accuracy and damage, and the result is shown.

First to hit 0 HP loses the pot.

docs.title: Required Fields
docs.text:
• opponent - Discord member to challenge.
  • bet - Amount each player puts up.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /fight [opponent] [bet]

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /fight @username 100k /fight @username 5m /fight @username max

docs.title: Weapons
docs.text:
Weapon Damage Accuracy Crit % Notes ⚔️ Sword 20–34 85% 20% Balanced, consistent 🪓 Battle Axe 25–39 75% 25% Heavy hitter, less accurate 🔱 Spear 15–29 90% 15% Reliable but lower damage 🏹 Bow 20–44 80% 30% High variance 🥿 Mom's Slipper 12–25 85% 25% Surprisingly competitive 🗡️ Dagger 8–18 95% 40% Crit-fishing build 🪄 Magic Staff 15–19 100% 10% Always hits; one move heals 15 HP 🛡️ Sword + Shield 12–24 80% 15% Tank-style, lower damage

Both players also see a help embed before the duel showing all weapon stats.

docs.title: Combat math
docs.text:
• Accuracy check runs first - a miss does 0 damage.
  • Crit roll doubles damage on success.
  • Damage is uniform-random within the weapon's range.
  • Some moves modify accuracy/damage temporarily (per-weapon move lists).

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 4 seconds (with internal rate limiter on top - spam protection).
  • Min bet: $10,000.
  • No max bet cap - max confirms before locking in your full balance.
  • Only one fight per channel at a time.
  • HP starts at 100 for both fighters; first to 0 loses.
  • For pure-luck 1v1s, see /coinflip-duel, /diceduel, or /russian-roulette.

docs.title: /multiblackjack
docs.text: Multi-seat blackjack table - up to 7 players sharing one dealer. Each player plays their own hand independently against the same dealer.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
The host opens a lobby with their bet. Up to 7 total players can join (each picks their own bet). When the table starts, every player is dealt their own hand. Players take turns hitting/standing/doubling/splitting against a single shared dealer.

Each player wins or loses their own bet independently against the dealer - beating other players doesn't matter. Standard blackjack rules apply (3:2 BJ payout, dealer hits soft 17, splits/doubles allowed).

For solo blackjack, see /blackjack.

docs.title: Required Fields
docs.text: • bet - Host's bet (each joiner picks their own bet when joining).

docs.title: Optional Fields
docs.text:
• max_players - Maximum players allowed at the table (1–7, default 7).
  • autostart - Auto-start when the lobby timer ends if minimum players are met (default true).
  • min_autostart_players - Minimum players needed to autostart (1–7, default 1).

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /multiblackjack [bet] [max_players] [autostart] [min_autostart_players]

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /multiblackjack 100k /multiblackjack 1m max_players:4 /multiblackjack 5m max_players:7 autostart:true min_autostart_players:3

docs.title: Lobby flow
docs.text:
1. Host runs the command - host's bet is deducted and the lobby is posted.
  2. Other players click Join and pick their own bet.
  3. Lobby has a 5-minute countdown timer.
  4. Host can Start anytime; otherwise autostart fires when the timer ends if min_autostart_players is met.
  5. If the lobby expires with no players, all bets are refunded.

docs.title: Game flow
docs.text:
1. Each player is dealt 2 cards (their hand is theirs alone).
  2. Dealer shows one face-up + one hole card.
  3. Players take turns acting on their hand: Hit / Stand / Double / Split.
  4. After all players finish, the dealer plays out (hits soft 17).
  5. Each player's hand is settled independently against the dealer's final hand.

docs.title: Payouts
docs.text:
Result Payout Beat the dealer 1:1 Natural blackjack (and dealer doesn't have one) 3:2 Push (tie with dealer) Bet returned Dealer wins Lose bet

Splits and doubles use full extra bets and pay 1:1.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 5 seconds (host).
  • Players: 1–7 (max configurable per game).
  • Timer: 5-minute lobby with optional autostart.
  • Each player can only be in one game at a time - GameManager enforces this for everyone at the table.
  • Only one Multiplayer Blackjack per channel at a time.
  • On error, all bets are refunded automatically.
  • For solo play, see /blackjack.

docs.title: /multiplayer_dice
docs.text: Group dice elimination. Every round picks a "death number" - anyone who rolls it is out. Last player standing takes the entire pot.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
The host opens a lobby with an entry fee. Other players join and pay the same fee. Once the host hits Start (minimum 2 players), each round the bot picks a random elimination number 1–6 and rolls one die for every remaining player. Roll the death number and you're out.

Last player standing wins the full pot: entry_fee × number of players.

docs.title: Required Fields
docs.text: • entry_fee - Buy-in each player pays to join.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /multiplayer_dice [entry_fee]

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /multiplayer_dice 100k /multiplayer_dice 1m /multiplayer_dice max

docs.title: Lobby flow
docs.text:
1. Host runs the command - entry fee deducted, lobby embed posted.
  2. Other players see Join / Start / Cancel buttons.
  3. Host clicks Start when ready (minimum 2 players).
  4. If the host cancels, all entry fees are refunded.

docs.title: Round mechanics
docs.text:
Each round:

  1. Bot picks a random death number (1–6) with a suspense reveal.
  2. Every remaining player rolls one die.
  3. Anyone rolling the death number is eliminated.
  4. Survivors continue to the next round.
  5. Mercy round: if every remaining player rolls the death number, nobody is eliminated and the round repeats.

Repeat until only one player remains - they take the whole pot.

docs.title: Pot mechanics
docs.text:
• Each player pays entry_fee (deducted up-front).
  • Winner takes: entry_fee × total players.
  • Cancelled lobby = full refund to everyone.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Min entry fee: $10,000.
  • Max entry fee: standard MAX_BET (bet bypass users excepted).
  • Minimum 2 players to start.
  • Only one Multiplayer Dice game per channel at a time.
  • Host can't start their own game while already hosting another.
  • For solo dice, see /dice or /multidice.
  • For 1v1 dice duel, see /diceduel.

docs.title: /murdermystery
docs.text: Social-deduction party game for 4–15 players. Killers pick off the innocent team at night; detectives investigate; meetings vote out suspects. Last side standing splits the prize pot.

docs.title: Description
docs.text: A snappy Discord take on Among Us / Mafia. The host opens a lobby with an entry fee; players who join pay the same fee into a shared pot. Once the game starts, it alternates between night rounds (~2 min) and meetings (~2 min). A persistent status panel reposts itself to the bottom of the channel whenever something changes, so you never have to scroll back to find the buttons.

docs.title: Optional Fields
docs.text:
• entry_fee - Per-player buy-in (k, m, max, half supported; default 10k). Must meet the bot's minimum bet.
  • max_players - Maximum players in lobby (4–15, default 10).
  • killers - Number of killers (1–3, default 1).

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /murdermystery [entry_fee] [max_players] [killers]

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /murdermystery /murdermystery entry_fee:50k /murdermystery entry_fee:1m max_players:12 killers:2 /murdermystery max_players:15 killers:3

docs.title: Free NPC test
docs.text:
/murdermystery-npctest [npc_count] [killers] [your_role]

Free practice mode against NPC players. NPC killers and guardian angels auto-play; other NPC roles idle (so NPC detectives/doctors are basically wasted slots).

  • npc_count - Number of fake players (3–14, default 7).
  • killers - Number of killers (1–3, default 1).
  • your_role - Force your own role (random, killer, detective, guardian_angel, doctor, juggernaut, civilian).

docs.title: Player requirements
docs.text: Killers Minimum players 1 4 2 6 3 7

docs.title: Roles
docs.text:
• Killer - Picks one player to eliminate each night. 10-second wait after the round starts before the first kill of the round.
  • Detective - Investigates a player and posts a public read: SUSPICIOUS or INNOCENT. Reads have a 15% chance to flip, so cross-check before voting. Investigations per round = max(2, alive // 5).
  • Guardian Angel - Picks one player to protect each night. A killer's attack on the protected player is blocked. Available at 5+ players.
  • Doctor - Once per game, revive a dead player. Available at 6+ players.
  • Juggernaut - Automatically survives the first two killer attacks. Still vulnerable to vote-outs. Available at 7+ players.
  • Civilian - No special powers; discuss, vote, survive.

Detectives are assigned 1 per 6 players (minimum 1). One guardian angel / doctor / juggernaut slot is added as the lobby grows past the thresholds above.

docs.title: Game flow
docs.text:
1. Lobby - players join via button; host starts manually. Lobby expires after 10 minutes of inactivity, refunding all entry fees.
  2. Role reveal - each player gets their role by DM (ephemeral fallback if DMs are closed). Night begins after a short 5-second pause.
  3. Night round (~2 min) - killers/detectives/guardian angels/doctors act. The status panel reposts on every action. Round ends early when all alive killers have acted.
  4. Meeting (~2 min) - 30 seconds of discussion, then ~75 seconds of voting. Players can skip or vote a target. Live vote tally shown on the panel.
  5. Repeat night/meeting until one side wins.
  6. Auto-cleanup if a game runs longer than 60 minutes; entry fees and unsettled penalties are refunded.

docs.title: Status panel
docs.text:
Instead of scattered action messages, the game shows a single status panel that gets reposted whenever something happens. It always shows:

  • Current phase (Night N, Discussion, or Voting)
  • Alive / dead counts, killers remaining, prize pot
  • Live vote tally during meetings
  • Buttons for whatever action makes sense in this phase

When discussion bumps the panel up the channel, an action (kill, vote, etc.) automatically reposts it at the bottom.

docs.title: Economy
docs.text:
• Entry fee is deducted on join and added to the pot.
  • Leaving the lobby before start refunds your fee.
  • Wrongful-vote penalty: if the meeting eliminates an innocent player, every innocent voter who voted for them is fined max(1, entry_fee / 4) from their wallet (capped at current balance). The fine is added to the pot.
  • Catching a killer: voters credited with eliminating a killer earn "catch" credit, boosting their share of the final pot.
  • NPC test mode uses no money — no penalties, no payouts.

docs.title: Win conditions
docs.text:
• Innocents win when all killers are eliminated.
  • Killers win when alive killers ≥ alive innocents.

docs.title: Reward split
docs.text:
The pot is split among human winners by score (NPCs never collect):

  • Innocents score = 1 + (catches × 3) + (1 if alive) − wrong_votes, floored at 1.
  • Killers score = 1 + (kills × 2) + (1 if alive), floored at 1.

Each winner gets pot × score / total_score, with any remainder going to the top scorer.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 5 seconds (host).
  • One game per channel at a time.
  • Lobby and game both auto-clean if abandoned, refunding fees and penalties.
  • The host can leave during the lobby; host transfers to the next remaining player.
  • Re-DM your role any time with the Get Role Info button.

docs.title: /rps-multiplayer
docs.text: Challenge another player to Rock Paper Scissors. Both players ante up the same bet, pick their move privately, and the winner takes the pot.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Standard Rock Paper Scissors against a real opponent - no bot weighting, no edge. Both players are charged the bet up front. After accepting, each player picks Rock, Paper, or Scissors privately. Winner takes bet × 2; ties refund both bets.

For the solo version against the bot, see /rps.

docs.title: Required Fields
docs.text:
• opponent - Discord member to challenge.
  • bet - Amount each player puts up.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /rps-multiplayer [opponent] [bet]

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /rps-multiplayer @username 100k /rps-multiplayer @username 5m /rps-multiplayer @username max

docs.title: Match flow
docs.text:
1. Challenge sent - opponent has 30 seconds to Accept or Decline.
  2. Both wallets are checked for the bet amount before the challenge can fire.
  3. After acceptance, both players pick their move via private buttons.
  4. Moves revealed simultaneously.
  5. Winner takes bet × 2; tie = both refunded.

docs.title: Outcomes
docs.text: Your move Opponent's move Result Rock Scissors Win Paper Rock Win Scissors Paper Win Same as opponent - Tie (refund) Rock Paper Loss Paper Scissors Loss Scissors Rock Loss

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 4 seconds.
  • Min bet: $10,000.
  • No max bet cap - max confirms before locking in your full balance.
  • Both players' wallets must cover the bet before the challenge sends.
  • Each player can only be in one Rps-Multiplayer game at a time.
  • For the solo version, see /rps.

docs.title: /russian-roulette
docs.text: Group elimination shootout. Players take turns pulling the trigger - survive long enough and you take the entire pot.

docs.title: Description
docs.text: You start a lobby in your channel with an entry fee. Other players join by clicking. Once the host starts the game (or autostart fires), each round the bot picks a player at random to "pull the trigger." Eliminated players are out; last one standing wins the pot.

docs.title: Required Fields
docs.text: • entry_fee - Buy-in amount each player pays.

docs.title: Optional Fields
docs.text:
• max_players - Cap the number of players (default 0 = unlimited).
  • autostart - Auto-start the game when the lobby timer ends if minimum players are met (default false).
  • min_autostart_players - Minimum players required to autostart (default 2, must be 2+).

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /russian-roulette [entry_fee] [max_players] [autostart] [min_autostart_players]

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /russian-roulette 100k /russian-roulette 1m max_players:6 autostart:true /russian-roulette 5m max_players:0 autostart:true min_autostart_players:3

docs.title: Lobby flow
docs.text:
1. Host runs the command - entry fee deducted, lobby embed posted.
  2. Other players see Join / Leave / Start / Cancel buttons.
  3. Lobby auto-cleans after 5 minutes of inactivity.
  4. Host clicks Start when ready (or autostart fires).
  5. Bot rolls for each round, eliminates players one-by-one, posts the survivor as the pot winner.

docs.title: Pot mechanics
docs.text:
• Each player who joins pays entry_fee (deducted up-front).
  • Winner takes the full pot: entry_fee × number of joiners.
  • If the lobby is canceled, all entry fees are refunded.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 4 seconds (host).
  • Min entry fee: $10,000.
  • No max bet cap.
  • Only one Russian Roulette per channel at a time.
  • See /russian-roulette-stats for personal kill/survive stats (run /russian-roulette-stats directly).
  • For 1v1 nerve games, see /buckshot play instead - shorter and more strategic.

docs.title: /simple-buckshot
docs.text: Group battle-royale gun game. A lobby opens, anyone can join, and players take turns passing a loaded gun until one person is left standing.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
/simple-buckshot [entry_fee] [max_players]

  • entry_fee - Wager each player pays to enter. Deducted on join.
  • max_players (optional) - Cap the lobby size. 0 = unlimited.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /simple-buckshot 50k /simple-buckshot 1m 8

docs.title: How it works
docs.text:
1. Host runs /simple-buckshot; a lobby embed opens with Join / Leave / Start / Cancel buttons.
  2. Lobby stays open for up to 5 minutes — minimum 2 players required to start.
  3. Entry fee is deducted from each player when they join. Pot = entry_fee × player count.
  4. Once started, players take turns in order. Each turn the gun fires with ~1/3 live shell probability.
  5. Live → that player is eliminated.
  6. Blank → nothing happens, turn passes.
  7. Eliminated players are out. Last survivor wins the entire pot.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Min bet: $10,000. No max bet cap.
  • Lobby expires after 5 minutes with fewer than 2 players — all entry fees are refunded.
  • One active game per channel at a time.
  • This is a separate game from /buckshot (the 1v1 shotgun strategy game with items).

docs.title: /tic-tac-toe
docs.text: Classic 3×3 tic-tac-toe against another player. Get three in a row to win the pot.

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Standard tic-tac-toe in a Discord button grid. Players alternate placing X and O on a 3×3 board. First to get three in a row (horizontal, vertical, or diagonal) wins. Full board with no winner = draw, bets refunded.

docs.title: Required Fields
docs.text:
• opponent - Discord member to challenge.
  • bet - Amount each player puts up.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: /tic-tac-toe [opponent] [bet]

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /tic-tac-toe @username 100k /tic-tac-toe @username 5m /tic-tac-toe @username max

docs.title: Match flow
docs.text:
1. Challenge sent - opponent has 30 seconds to accept.
  2. Players alternate clicking buttons on a 3×3 grid.
  3. First to line up 3 wins the pot (bet × 2).
  4. Tie = both players get their bets back.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cooldown: 4 seconds.
  • Min bet: $10,000.
  • No max bet cap - set bet: max to wager everything (a confirmation prompt appears).
  • Both wallets must cover the bet to start.
  • Tic-tac-toe is solved game theory - perfect play always ends in a draw, so this is mostly a quick-money game between unfocused or beginner opponents.
  • For other 1v1 board games, see /connect4, /battleship, /chess.

docs.title: /wordle
docs.text: Guess a 5-letter word in 6 tries. Solo, daily, or duel another player for a pot.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Paradise Bot's Wordle is a SlashCommandGroup - /wordle <subcommand> - with multiple game modes:

  • Solo - practice against the bot, no money.
  • Daily - one shared puzzle per day, with a daily leaderboard.
  • Duel - head-to-head against another player, optional money on the line.
  • Stats / leaderboard - view records.

Letter feedback is the standard Wordle palette: 🟩 right letter right position, 🟨 right letter wrong position, ⬛ wrong letter.

docs.title: Subcommands
docs.text: Subcommand Description Cooldown /wordle solo Solo practice game 3s /wordle daily Today's daily puzzle (shared word) 5s /wordle duel @user [bet] Challenge another player 5s /wordle daily-leaderboard Today's daily-puzzle ranks 5s /wordle daily-stats [@user] Daily Wordle stats for a user 5s /wordle stats [@user] Overall Wordle stats for a user 5s /wordle leaderboard Global Wordle leaderboard 5s

docs.title: /wordle duel
docs.text:
/wordle duel [opponent] [bet]

  • opponent - Discord member to challenge.
  • bet (optional, default 0) - Amount each player puts up. 0 for a friendly match with no money.

Both players guess the same word; first to solve in fewer guesses wins the pot. Same number of guesses = the player who solved first wins.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: /wordle duel @username - friendly duel /wordle duel @username 100k /wordle duel @username max

docs.title: Daily mode
docs.text:
• One puzzle per day, same word for everyone.
  • Builds a daily streak counter (consecutive days solved).
  • Top solvers are listed on /wordle daily-leaderboard after they finish.

docs.title: Stats tracked
docs.text:
Per user (via /wordle stats):

  • Games played, win rate, current win streak, best win streak
  • Best guess count (lowest), average guesses on wins
  • Total wagered, total won, total lost
  • Daily mode: solve rate, fastest solve time, daily streak, best daily streak

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Min bet (duel with bet > 0): $10,000.
  • No max bet cap - max confirms before locking in your full balance.
  • Each player can only be in one Wordle game at a time.
  • Daily mode resets at midnight UTC.
  • The word list is the standard Wordle answer pool.

docs.title: About Paradise Bot
docs.text: Paradise Bot is a Discord-first simulated gambling and economy bot with deep progression systems, multiplayer games, and an active development roadmap.

docs.title: What it does
docs.text:
Paradise Bot ties together a wide range of mechanics in one place:

  • 16 casino games - blackjack, poker, slots, roulette, baccarat, dice, crash, tower, minesweeper, plinko, wheel-of-fortune, horse-race, sportsbet, uncrossable, RPS, coinflip.
  • 14 multiplayer games - battleship, connect4, tic-tac-toe, wordle, fight, coinflip duel, dice duel, russian roulette, buckshot, murder mystery, RPS multiplayer, multiplayer dice, multidice, multiplayer blackjack.
  • Progression systems - pets (level 1–200), mining (14 pickaxes / 13 depths), fishing (13 rods / 13 locations), farming (11 crops with real-time growth).
  • Heists - co-op multiplayer + solo, with 8 locations and a daily Weekly Major Heist.
  • Restaurants - Fries delivery minigame.
  • Lottery - daily community drawing fueled by ticket sales and game taxes.
  • Banking - wallet vs bank split, daily interest, 15 vault tiers.
  • Scratch cards from voting + Patreon perks across the entire bot.

docs.title: Built by
docs.text: Mal023 (one developer). Active development since 2024+. Hosted in the Paradise community Discord with a small staff team for moderation and Patreon management.

docs.title: Tech
docs.text:
• py-cord - Discord bot library.
  • SQLite - sharded databases for performance (e.g., farming has 10 shards).
  • Python 3.14 runtime.
  • MkDocs Material for this wiki.

docs.title: Where to start
docs.text:
• New player? Run /guide for the interactive tour.
  • Need money fast? /freemoney lists every income command.
  • Want to gamble? /games shows everything available.
  • Support the bot? Patreon and /donate.

docs.title: Language
docs.text: Paradise Bot supports multiple languages. You can set a language for yourself or, if you're an admin, for your whole server.

docs.title: Available Languages
docs.text: Language Code English (default) en Español es-ES Français fr Nederlands nl Deutsch de Português (Brasil) pt-BR

docs.title: Commands
docs.text: Command Who can use What it does /language user <language> Anyone Set your personal language /language server <language> Admins only Set the server-wide language /language reset user Anyone Clear your personal setting /language reset server Admins only Clear the server setting /language current Anyone Show your current language settings

docs.title: Priority
docs.text:
Your personal language always wins. If you haven't set one, the server's language is used. If neither is set, the bot defaults to English.

Personal setting → Server setting → English (default)

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Personal settings are account-wide — they follow you across all servers.
  • Server settings apply to all users in that server who haven't set a personal preference.
  • Multiplayer game embeds (shared with all players) use the server language.

docs.title: Rules
docs.text: Paradise Bot is a fair-play environment. These rules cover what's not allowed and what gets you banned.

docs.title: Hard bans (immediate)
docs.text:
• Exploit abuse - duping money, abusing race conditions, or otherwise gaming bugs in the bot's economy.
  • Selfbots / automation - running scripts that auto-spam commands, click buttons, or chain rewards.
  • Multi-accounting - using multiple Discord accounts to farm voting rewards, daily, etc.
  • Real-money trading (RMT) - selling in-game currency for real money is forbidden.
  • Harassment / abuse in the Paradise support server.

docs.title: Soft warnings → temporary bans
docs.text:
• Begging in non-beg channels for free money from staff.
  • Spamming the same command repeatedly to clog the bot.
  • Failing captcha (/verify) repeatedly may lead to a temporary lock.

docs.title: Fair play
docs.text:
• One account per person. Alts will be banned (yours and the alt).
  • Bot uptime is best-effort. No SLA is guaranteed.
  • Maintenance mode can lock commands during updates - wait it out.

docs.title: Refunds
docs.text:
• Patreon donations are non-refundable. They support development; they don't entitle you to refunds for losing streaks.
  • In-game money lost in games is not refundable. That's how gambling works.
  • Bug-related refunds are case-by-case - open a support ticket.

docs.title: Anti-selfbot
docs.text: The bot runs random captchas via /verify. Failing or skipping these can flag your account.

docs.title: Reporting violations
docs.text:
• Open a ticket in the support Discord (use /links).
  • Provide the offending user's Discord ID and screenshots if relevant.
  • Staff reviews reports manually.

docs.title: Frequently Asked Questions
docs.text: Quick answers to common Paradise Bot questions.

docs.title: Q: How do I get started with Paradise Bot?
docs.text: A: Use /daily to get starting money, then try simple games like /coinflip 100 heads. Check /help for more commands.

docs.title: Q: Why can't I use commands?
docs.text: A: Make sure the bot has proper permissions and you're in an allowed channel. Contact your server admin if issues persist.

docs.title: Q: How do I get more money?
docs.text: A: Use /daily and /hourly for free money. Play games, complete jobs, try farming, or participate in heists for more earnings.

docs.title: Q: Can I lose money I put in the bank?
docs.text: A: No! Money in your bank account is completely safe and cannot be lost through gambling or other activities.

docs.title: Q: What's the daily limit for gambling?
docs.text: A: This varies by server settings. Use /limits to check your current limits.

docs.title: Q: Can I trade items with other players?
docs.text: A: Yes! Use /trade @user to initiate trades. Both players must confirm before the trade completes.

docs.title: Q: Do I earn interest on bank money?
docs.text: A: This depends on your server's configuration. Some servers offer small daily interest on banked funds.

docs.title: Q: Are the games fair?
docs.text: A: Yes! All games use certified random number generation.

docs.title: Q: Why did I lose a coin flip?
docs.text: A: Coin flips are 50/50 chance. Losing streaks happen naturally - that's just variance.

docs.title: Q: Can I play games against other players?
docs.text: A: Absolutely! Try /duel @user, /fight @user, or join a /heist for multiplayer fun.

docs.title: Q: What's the maximum bet?
docs.text: A: Maximum bets vary by game and server settings. Use /help <game> to see specific limits.

docs.title: Q: The bot isn't responding to my commands
docs.text: A: Try these steps: 1. Make sure you're using slash commands (/ prefix) 2. Check if the bot is online (green status) 3. Verify the bot has message permissions 4. Try in a different channel

docs.title: Q: My balance disappeared!
docs.text: A: Balances don't disappear without reason. Check: - Recent game history with /history - If someone robbed you (check notifications) - Server-wide economy events or resets

docs.title: Q: Commands are slow or timing out
docs.text: A: This usually indicates high bot load or Discord API issues. Wait a few minutes and try again.

docs.title: Q: How do heists work?
docs.text: A: Heists are group activities. Use /heist join when someone starts one. Success depends on team size and luck.

docs.title: Q: Can I give money to friends?
docs.text: A: Yes! Use /pay @user <amount> to send money directly to other players.

docs.title: Q: How do I report inappropriate behavior?
docs.text: A: Use /report @user <reason> or contact server moderators directly.

docs.title: Q: What are properties and how do they work?
docs.text: A: Properties provide passive income. Buy them with /property buy <name> and earn daily rent payments.

docs.title: Q: How does farming work?
docs.text: A: Plant crops with /farm plant <crop>, wait for them to grow, then harvest for profit. Different crops have different growth times and values.

docs.title: Q: Can I automate activities?
docs.text: A: No, all commands must be run manually. Automation/botting is against the rules and can result in penalties.

docs.title: Q: What happens if I break the rules?
docs.text: A: Violations can result in temporary mutes, balance reductions, or permanent bans depending on severity.

docs.title: Q: Is my data safe?
docs.text: A: Yes, we follow Discord's privacy policies and don't store unnecessary personal information.

docs.title: Q: Can I have multiple accounts?
docs.text: A: No, one account per person is allowed. Multi-accounting results in all accounts being banned.

docs.title: Still Need Help?
docs.text:
If your question isn't answered here:

  1. Check the help command: /help <topic>
  2. Ask in your server: Other users might help
  3. Contact support: Get Support →
  4. Report bugs: Use /bug <description>

Updated regularly based on common user questions.

docs.title: Report Bugs
docs.text: Found a bug? Use /bugreport directly inside Discord - it sends to the dev's bug-report channel automatically.

docs.title: /bugreport
docs.text: /bugreport bug:"<description>" [attachment]

docs.title: Required Fields
docs.text: • bug - Description of the bug. Be specific.

docs.title: Optional Fields
docs.text: • attachment - Image (screenshot) of the bug. Image attachments only.

docs.title: Example
docs.text: /bugreport bug:"/baccarat shows wrong payout when betting on Banker - paid 1.95× but said 2× in result embed"

docs.title: What to include
docs.text:
A good bug report has:

  • What you did - exact command + arguments.
  • What you expected.
  • What actually happened.
  • Screenshot if visual.
  • Approximate time when it happened (helps with logs).

docs.title: What happens after
docs.text:
• Your report goes to the dev's bug channel.
  • The bot replies "Your bug report has been sent to the developer."
  • The dev reviews reports manually.
  • If your report is fixed/wontfix/duplicate, the dev may notify you in the support server.

docs.title: Cooldown
docs.text: /bugreport has a 60-second cooldown to prevent spam. Don't submit the same bug twice - even if your first one didn't get an immediate reply, it was received.

docs.title: Don't use /bugreport for…
docs.text:
• Suggestions - use /suggest instead.
  • Personal account issues (lost balance, missing rewards) - open a support ticket in Discord.
  • Asking for help with how to play - use /guide or the wiki.

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• Suggestions
  • Ask for Help
  • Github - for tracked issues.

docs.title: Suggestions
docs.text: Have an idea for a new game, feature, or improvement? Use /suggest - it goes straight to the dev's suggestion channel and creates a discussion thread.

docs.title: /suggest
docs.text: /suggest suggestion:"<your idea>" [attachment]

docs.title: Required Fields
docs.text: • suggestion - Your idea. The more specific, the better.

docs.title: Optional Fields
docs.text: • attachment - Image to help explain. Image attachments only.

docs.title: Example
docs.text: /suggest suggestion:"Add a /lottery-history command that shows my past tickets and whether they won"

docs.title: What happens after
docs.text:
1. Your suggestion is posted in the suggestions channel.
  2. Two reactions are added automatically: 👍 and 👎 - community voting.
  3. A discussion thread is auto-created (7-day archive timer) where staff and community can debate and refine the idea.
  4. The dev reviews popular/well-discussed suggestions periodically.

docs.title: Tips for good suggestions
docs.text:
• One idea per /suggest - don't pack 10 features into one submission.
  • Explain why - what problem does this solve, who benefits?
  • Be specific - "add more variety" is too vague; "add a /poker tournament mode with brackets" is actionable.
  • Search the suggestions channel first - if your idea has already been suggested, react/comment instead of duplicating.

docs.title: Cooldown
docs.text: /suggest has a 5-minute cooldown (300 seconds). Take your time - write a clear suggestion rather than spamming half-baked ones.

docs.title: Don't use /suggest for…
docs.text:
• Bugs - use /bugreport instead.
  • Personal payment issues - open a Discord ticket.
  • Complaints - use the appropriate support channel.

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• Report Bugs
  • Ask for Help
  • Discord support server

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: Common Paradise Bot issues and how to fix them.

docs.title: Bot Isn't Responding
docs.text:
Check the basics first:

  1. Use slash commands - All commands start with /. The bot does not respond to text prefixes.
  2. Bot is online - Look for the green status dot next to the bot's name.
  3. Permissions - The bot needs Send Messages, Embed Links, Use Slash Commands, and Read Message History in the channel.
  4. Right channel - Some servers restrict bot use to specific channels. Ask an admin if you're unsure.
  5. Try a different channel - Rules out a channel-specific permission issue.

If the bot still doesn't respond, it may be down. Check the support server for status.

docs.title: My Balance Looks Wrong
docs.text:
Balances don't change without a reason. Check:

  • Recent activity - /history shows your last transactions
  • You got robbed - Other players can use /rob @you. Check notifications.
  • Server-wide events - Some servers run economy resets or bonus events.
  • Property/farm taxes - Recurring upkeep is deducted automatically.

If you're sure something is wrong, file /bug <description> with as much detail as possible.

docs.title: Commands Are Slow or Time Out
docs.text:
This usually means high load - either Discord's API or the bot itself.

  • Wait a minute and try again
  • Check the support server for known incidents
  • Avoid spamming the same command - it makes things worse

docs.title: "You're on cooldown"
docs.text: Most income commands (/daily, /hourly, /beg, /crime) have cooldowns. The error message tells you when you can run them next. Patrons get reduced cooldowns.

docs.title: A Specific Game Is Broken
docs.text:
Use /bug <description> and include:

  • The game and command you ran
  • What you expected to happen
  • What actually happened
  • Approximate time

Screenshots help. If the game ate your money, the developer can usually refund once verified.

docs.title: "You don't have permission"
docs.text:
Some commands are admin-only. If you think you should have access:

  • Ask a server admin to check the bot's role configuration
  • Some commands require Administrator or Manage Server Discord permissions

docs.title: I Was Banned by Mistake
docs.text: Open a ticket in the support server. Include your Discord ID and a brief explanation. Bans aren't reversed without context, so be specific.
